posts.title: The Only Minimalisztic Night Skincare Routine You Will Ever Need
posts.excerpt: Dermatologists often say that your sleep is a regenerative process; your skin undergoes a self-repair process. Your skin refreshes itself and regenerates new collagen cells...
posts.content:
There’s no better feeling in the world than getting into bed at the end of a long and stressful day. But, if you are walking to your bed the minute you get home without taking off the day’s makeup or pollution, then it’s high time we said something! The lack of proper care likely means you wake up with dull, lackluster skin that appears tired.

Dermatologists often say that your sleep is a regenerative process; your skin undergoes a self-repair process. Your skin refreshes itself and regenerates new collagen cells faster during the night, when we are away from dust, pollution, and the sun.

There is an increase in blood flow in your skin at night because you are finally resting your body and skin after a long day. This improves the effectiveness of your cosmetic products on your skin.

A nightly skincare routine that focuses on hydration and repair can help maintain the firmness of your skin. As a result, following a proper and effective night skincare regimen is essential if you want to wake up with fresh, rejuvenated, youthful, and glowing skin.

What is the Difference between a Night and Morning Skincare Routine?

Your morning skincare routine is all about protection. You are protecting your skin from all the pollutants, sun damage, and impurities that you are sure to encounter throughout the day. You use preventative products that shield you from any permanent harm done to your face.

On the other hand, your skin absorbs the benefits of skin care better at night. While you sleep, your skin regenerates and is better able to absorb nutrients, ingredients, and minerals.

There is no penetration barrier, and your skin is able to breathe more readily at night. Hence, your nighttime skincare routine is the ideal time to actively combat any skin issues, especially anti-aging.

Must-Haves in your Nighttime Minimalistic Skincare Routine

Battle the Makeup

Going straight to bed without taking off can clog your pores & result in skin damage. As a result, the very first step in your evening skincare process should always be to remove any makeup. This is the most crucial phase in your nighttime skincare routine.

Given the resistant nature of most cosmetic products today, just cleansing your face with warm water may not be adequate. It is preferable to use a gentle makeup remover.

Empress Pro-Tip

Double Cleansing with an oil cleanser to take makeup off is the best way to make sure your skin is makeup-free before you treat it to all of your luxurious skincare. In a pinch, you can massage some coconut oil on your face till it melts your makeup away and follow it up with the next step!


Cleansing

The second step in your nighttime regimen is to actually cleanse your skin. Wash away dirt, pollutants, oil, and residual makeup using a gentle cleanser (this is different from the oil-based cleansing you do in Step 1).

Pick a cleanser that is made from natural ingredients and is free of synthetic chemicals for your skin type. Cleansers with chemicals will irritate your skin instead of cleaning it and end up worsening any skin problems you already have.


Try your own DIY natural cleanser:

Ingredients:

  • Powdered rice flour (for acne-prone or sensitive skin)
  • Powdered coconut milk (for dry skin)
  • Rose/Distilled water

In your hand, combine a teaspoon of the powder that suits your skin type with distilled water or rose water and proceed to gently massage the mixture into your skin.

Moisturization

After the initial two stages of your night skincare routine, your skin may get dry. To bring it back to its glowing state, the following step in the night care regimen is to replenish it with moisturizer.

Moisturization is an essential component in every skin care routine, whether morning or night. It nourishes your skin from within and aids in their repair and regeneration over time.

The right moisturizer for your skin would be one that is free of chemicals and sinks right into your skin. Make sure it is lightweight, non-greasy, and leaves your skin feeling nourished.

If you’re having trouble finding the right moisturizer for you, try our holy-grail Luxe Night Elixir.

It contains an amalgamation of seven of the best facial oils for your skin, including Rosehip Seed Oil, which is known for its antioxidant, nourishing, and anti-aging properties.

It doesn’t clog your pores and instead sinks right into your skin, imparting a glow from within!

Don’t forget your body

Your nighttime skincare routine mustn't stop at facial care. Your body skin is exposed to sunlight, pollution, etc. just as much as your face. Moreover, throughout the day, your hands and legs are the most vulnerable to dryness and dehydration. Massage your skin with either a body moisturizer or body oil.

Our CEO swears by our Calming Massage Oil because it does so many things for your skin with just one step. It is an anti-inflammatory, anti-stress, and anxiety-fighting massage oil infused with Lavender, Ylang Ylang, and Sweet Marjoram.

Not only does it help nourish your body from the outside, but it also offers relaxation like never before! Going to bed after your self-massage will help you fall asleep more easily and have a peaceful night of rest.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQS) about building your routine

When is the right time to do my night skincare routine?

The best time to do your nighttime routine is right before you go to bed. It can help you unwind and reflect on all that you did throughout the day.

Also, the calming nature of the routine will help you fall asleep faster, especially if you are using the right products. Your skin repairs itself at night, so doing your routine right before you get into bed will maximize the potential of all the products you use.

How complicated does my routine need to be?

We are strong believers in the power of a minimal and clean night skincare routine. Too many activities can do more damage than good to your skin. We suggest you stick to a simple routine of cleansing and moisturizing with simple actives targeting your skin concerns.

Check out our Buyer’s Guide to know more about the actives we use and why they are the best for mature skin.

Fortnightly, you can also use clay or sheet masks to pamper yourself and your skin more.

Are there any tools/devices that I need?

For the most part, your hands are all you need. Our CEO, Elizabeth, loves using her Kansa Wand after applying her serum to massage her face and aid lymphatic drainage. Once in a while, she also uses a “Thera gun massager" on her legs and neck for a deep tissue body massage.

Is consistency important?

Absolutely! Consistency is key when it comes to developing any skincare routine. Building consistency will help you associate your nighttime routine with an unwinding and meditative state.

In fact, consistency is a must if you have mature skin, especially if you want to see results in your skin!

Any other tips for my nighttime routine?

Don’t forget that your nighttime routine is not only about your skin. Take that time to unwind your whole body.

Elizabeth’s ‘Restful Sleep’ Tea Blend

Combine skullcap, lavender, lemon balm, and alfalfa to make a tea that gives you the best sleep every night!

Secondly, don't forget to show some love to your hair once in a while. Oil your hair using Rosehip Seed Oil, Prickly Pear Seed Oil, or simply coconut oil, and rinse it after an hour or two.

It can be your weekend ritual, and trust us, your hair will love it!

posts.title: Step Up Your Menopausal Skincare and Wellness Routine: 3 Must-Haves
posts.excerpt: With so many changes occurring during menopause, your body is bound to experience visible changes, especially with respect to skin health. But that doesn't mean...
posts.content:
Have you ever heard the nickname often used in conversation about menopause: 'The Change'?

Well, it's not unheard of and definitely not inaccurate. It's no secret that menopause is one of the biggest biological changes you go through in your entire life. Your routine, holy-grail products, attitude on wellness - all can change faster than you realize; simply because your body needs care in a different way!

It's important to have a positive attitude towards menopause - but that doesn't magically get rid of the very real symptoms that accompany it.

But first, here’s how you know you are menopausal - in the absence of other symptoms, skipping your period for a year means you have entered menopause. Typically, women are seen to enter menopause in their 50s.

That’s when most of the not-so-fun symptoms start creeping up. Heat flashes, night chills, and irregular periods come into play, as they're all indicators that your ovaries are producing less estrogen.

With so many changes occurring internally, your body is bound to experience visible changes, especially with respect to skin health. But that doesn't mean all is lost!

We’re looking at the best skin and wellness practices to help smoothen and maintain your transition into menopause, filled with the best tips and tricks.

From DIY recipes to holy-grail products that will make your life so much easier without compromising on health, we’ve got you covered!

Understanding Skin Changes During Menopause

As you get older, your skin's epidermis often alters due to a combination of extrinsic or intrinsic factors. Pollution, stress, and sun exposure are examples of extrinsic variables that can cause your skin to age.

In contrast, inherent variables that cause skin to age over time include collagen loss. Collagen is the protein that gives our skin its structure and elasticity, so losing it over time can cause our skin to appear and feel older.

As you progress from perimenopause to menopause, your body produces less estrogen in your ovaries while producing more androgens, or masculine sex hormones like testosterone.

Estrogen stimulates the production of collagen and elasticity in our bodies. These proteins are necessary for keeping your epidermis moisturized and healthy. As a consequence of this abrupt imbalance, you have dry skin on your face, body, and just about anywhere else.

The loss of skin hydration and decreased collagen synthesis can make a significant difference in the look of the face. Once hormones start to decrease, the skin becomes thinner, wrinkled, and saggy.

But enough about this change! We all know it is bound to happen at some point. What we need to learn more about is how we can alter our routine to better facilitate the change and maintain our skin and overall health. After all, the aim is not to stop or reverse menopause but learn how to adapt and benefit from it.

Our Expert's Approach to Menopause Skincare

We asked our resident expert and CEO, Elizabeth Koshy, on her insider tips and tricks on how she takes care of her skin and body in her wellness journey.

We've got her must-have skincare and secrets that help her maintain a consistent menopause routine.

Elizabeth's Golden Rule: Keep It Simple, Keep It Minimalistic

Your skin is already going through a lot in how you live your daily life. Bombarding it with more products and action isn't helping its cause. Instead, cutting down your 12-step routine to more basic, natural ingredients that your skin actually needs will be a better value for your skin's health and your wallet.

Our 3-Step Skincare Routine For Menopause Skin

Step #1: Cleanse

The first step in any routine should be to cleanse your face, which might seem like a simple and obvious step but is often overlooked! Imagine trying to improve your health without getting rid of the root cause.

That's what treating skin that has not been cleaned properly is like! Our skin is subjected to so many pollutants and stresses during the day that cleansing it at night seems obvious.

The trouble here lies in picking the right cleanser that suits menopausal skin. Here are our recommendations for you to find the right cleanser for your skin type once you can identify its type!

Oily Skin: Use a gel cleanser to help eliminate excess grime from your face that may have accumulated during the day or night.

Acne-Prone Skin: Use a gentle wash with anti-blemish ingredients.

Dry Skin: If you have dry skin (which is extremely common during menopause!), search for a moisturizing oil or balm composition that includes ceramides and plant oils.

Want to try a DIY cleanser instead? We’ve got you covered!

Elizabeth makes her own blend of castor oil, coconut oil, pomegranate oil, raspberry oil, with ylang ylang essential oil for the perfect citrusy 'wake my skin' perfume. She uses this oil blend to soothe and cleanse her skin, which helps her combination-mature skin. Wipe with a clean, warm, wet towel, and you're good to go to nourish your skin further.

Step #2: Hydrate and Replenish

Cleansing strips your skin of all dirt and grime. But that means it also cleanses your skin of any protection and hydration. Now's the step where we replenish mature skin so it can continue to heal and glow!

Here's where the magic of a minimalistic routine comes in. The perfect formulations that give your skin all the nourishment it needs in one product. The right products will help you not only cut down on time and money but also not let you compromise your skin health in any way!

AM Morning Routine

Elizabeth uses Empress Naturals' AM Serum from the Serum Set in the morning. The simplest but effective blend of rosehip seed oil, CICA, this formulation is lightweight and provides your skin with the protection and hydration it needs every morning.

Your skin will never feel heavy or overburdened with this serum. It helps restore the radiance of the complexion throughout the day, and the vitamin A in rosehip seed oil works wonders for the main objective: anti-aging.

PM Night Routine

Your nighttime routine is to heal, repair, and nourish. Our PM Luxe Night Elixir is just that! Created to work while you rest, the PM Serum blends Circanblue™ postbiotic, Rosehip Oil, Pomegranate Oil, and Licorice Extract to support skin renewal and hormonal balance

Step #3: Don't Forget Your Body

It's more neglected than we like to think. We often think about all the changes happening to skin on your face, but seldom the body. Your body skin also needs to be nourished so you can feel hydrated and healthy all over. But maintaining skin health need not be complicated.

Your body wellness is one massage away!

We truly believe in the healing power of self-massages to help your body get the nutrition it needs and relaxation it deserves. There are multiple recipes that you can find online for the right kind of oils for self-massage.

But Elizabeth has very specific needs when it comes to taking care of her mature skin. That’s why she created the two massage oils she thinks every menopausal woman can benefit from - Our Calming Massage Oil and Circulation Massage Oil.

posts.title: Building a Sustainable Skincare Routine for Menopause and Perimenopause
posts.excerpt: Sustainable skincare is the antithesis of this wasteful mentality. It opposes overconsumption and can be perfectly applied to menopause skincare needs. Curating a minimalist, sustainable...
posts.content:
In this day and age, it can be easy to get caught up in all of the new and trendy skincare products being talked about all over social media. But when you're navigating the hormonal changes of perimenopause and menopause, your skin needs something more thoughtful than the latest viral trend.

While it may be fun experimenting with different products and purchasing the things we see gaining popularity online, this approach becomes particularly problematic during menopause, when your skin is undergoing significant changes. The idea that we go out and carelessly buy a bunch of the hottest serums and moisturizers on the market, rather than researching products that work best for our changing skin, is why so many women feel unsatisfied with their menopausal skincare results.

Sustainable skincare is the antithesis of this wasteful mentality. It opposes overconsumption and can be perfectly applied to menopause skincare needs. Curating a minimalist, sustainable skincare routine could be the very thing that is missing from your daily regimen, and you may realize that when you choose your products carefully and with intention, your skin will certainly thank you.

Minimalism Can Look Like…

Cutting down on the number of products that you use on your face. During menopause, your skin becomes more sensitive due to declining estrogen levels. Minimizing the amount you apply to your skin can make a significant difference if you're experiencing issues such as increased dryness, sensitivity, or hormonal breakouts. The reality is that just because you see influencers piling multiple serums onto their face does not mean that technique will work for menopausal skin that requires a gentler, more targeted approach.

Using clean products with purposeful ingredients. A huge aspect of sustainable skincare for menopause is using products that have clean, organic ingredients. Menopausal skin is more reactive and prone to irritation, making clean formulations essential. Products with fewer, high-quality ingredients deliver more of what your skin needs and less of the useless fillers that have no real benefit for your skin's changing biome.

Choosing products from ethical, sustainable brands. Sustainable menopause skincare means selecting products that are not only good for your skin but also good for the planet. Look for USDA Certified Organic ingredients, cruelty-free certifications, and brands that source ethically from farms across the globe.

Overall Benefits of a Sustainable Skincare Routine During Menopause

There are so many reasons why you should consider revamping your excessive routine to be minimal.

Your skin health will dramatically improve. One of the most desirable benefits of sustainable menopause skincare is how the overall health of your skin is sure to improve once you minimize your routine. During perimenopause and menopause, your skin loses collagen, becomes thinner, and retains less moisture. Stacking too many products only clogs your pores and prevents your skin from breathing and regenerating properly. Being intentional with the products you choose really teaches you about your changing skin—the things that work for you, and the things that do not.

You'll support your skin's natural barrier. Menopausal skin needs support in rebuilding and maintaining its protective barrier. Clean, organic ingredients work with your skin's natural processes rather than against them, helping to address the root causes of aging rather than just masking symptoms.

You will save money once you hone in on a small selection of targeted menopause products that are tailored to your needs and that you know work for you, rather than flocking to the store every time you hear of a new trendy product that is overpriced and underperforms.

posts.title: The Best Anti-Aging Ingredients for Menopause Skin
posts.excerpt: If you are wondering when you should start incorporating anti-aging skincare products into your routine, the answer is pretty straightforward - as soon as you...
posts.content:
When it comes to anti-aging, you have probably heard experts say ‘the earlier you start, the better’; but that doesn’t mean there is any right age to start using anti-aging skincare.

If you are wondering when you should start incorporating anti-aging skincare products into your routine, the answer is pretty straightforward - as soon as you can. But if you’re confused about how to find the right products, you’ve come to the right place.

Skincare can be tricky, especially when you feel lost in the sea of available products. Read on to know more about the right ingredients for your skin so your first, second or tenth try at anti-aging skincare can finally get it right!

Is Anti-Aging Skincare Necessary?

It’s no secret that as we age, our skin ages with us. We lose elasticity, develop fine lines and wrinkles, and are prone to excessive dryness due to the loss of collagen. Coupled with external factors like pollution and stress, your skin can age faster than you.

Here’s where anti-aging skincare helps save the day. Anti-aging skincare, especially products made from natural and clean ingredients, offers benefits that work beyond healing your skin from visible signs of aging. With the right ingredients and products, your anti-aging skincare routine could be the answer to most of your skin's woes; from hyperpigmentation to fine lines and wrinkles.

Read more about the benefits of incorporating anti-aging skincare into your routine

Finding the Best Ingredients in your Anti-Aging Skincare

Using effective and clean ingredients is the key to helping your skin stay nourished and healthy, especially as you age. Always remember, your products are only as good as their ingredients. Choosing the right ingredients is integral to helping your skin glow from within.

Rosehip Seed Oil

Our holy grail ingredient is an incredible multitasker for menopausal skin! Often known as the Queen of Natural Skincare, Rosehip Seed Oil sinks right into your skin, nourishing it from the inside out.

Rose hips have a high concentration of vitamin C, which boosts their antioxidant properties. They guard against UV-induced free radical damage, encourage collagen production (critical during menopause when collagen declines rapidly), and help with wrinkle reduction. The Vitamin A in Rosehip Oil may help your menopausal skin by reducing and correcting UV damage, reducing wrinkles, and treating hyperpigmentation and inflammation.

Find 100% Pure and Organic Rosehip Seed Oil in Empress Naturals' AM & PM Combo Set and Naked Collection: Liquid Gold

CICA (Centella Asiatica)

CICA, also known as Centella Asiatica or "tiger grass," is a powerhouse ingredient for sensitive, aging menopausal skin. This ancient herb has been used in traditional medicine for centuries and offers remarkable benefits for women navigating hormonal skin changes:

Strengthens Skin Barrier: During menopause, your skin barrier weakens significantly due to decreased estrogen levels. CICA helps repair and fortify this protective layer, reducing sensitivity and moisture loss that plague menopausal skin.

Boosts Collagen Production: CICA stimulates collagen synthesis, helping to combat the accelerated collagen loss that occurs during menopause. This makes it essential for maintaining skin firmness and elasticity.

Calms Inflammation: Menopausal skin often becomes more reactive and inflamed due to hormonal fluctuations. CICA's powerful anti-inflammatory properties soothe redness, irritation, and sensitivity.

Promotes Wound Healing: CICA accelerates skin repair and regeneration, helping your menopausal skin recover from environmental stressors more effectively while promoting a more even skin tone.

This clean, natural ingredient is perfect for women experiencing the increased sensitivity and inflammation that comes with menopause.

Find Centella Asiatica (CICA) in Empress Naturals' AM Serum.

Probiotic Lysate

Probiotic lysate is a breakthrough ingredient in anti-aging menopause skincare that works with your skin's natural microbiome to address hormonal skin changes:

Balances Skin Microbiome: During menopause, hormonal shifts disrupt your skin's natural bacterial balance, leading to increased sensitivity and breakouts. Probiotic lysate helps restore healthy flora, improving overall skin health and resilience.

Strengthens Immunity: It enhances your skin's natural defense mechanisms, making menopausal skin more resilient against environmental aggressors, irritants, and the inflammation that often accompanies hormonal changes.

Protects Against Blue Light Damage: In our digital age, menopausal skin is constantly exposed to blue light from screens, which can accelerate aging and cause oxidative stress. Probiotic lysate helps protect your skin from blue light damage, reducing inflammation and preventing premature aging caused by prolonged screen exposure.

Find our Probiotic Lysate in Empress Naturals' PM Serum.

Licorice Extract for Menopause Hyperpigmentation

Licorice extract is a gentle yet powerful botanical ingredient that addresses multiple menopausal skin concerns without irritation:

Brightens Hyperpigmentation: Menopause often triggers increased dark spots, age spots, and uneven skin tone due to hormonal fluctuations. Licorice extract contains glabridin, which inhibits melanin production and helps fade stubborn hyperpigmentation and melasma common during menopause.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: It soothes irritated, reactive menopausal skin, reduces redness, and calms the inflammation that often accompanies hormonal changes.

Antioxidant Protection: Licorice extract fights free radical damage, protecting your aging skin from environmental stressors that accelerate the visible signs of menopause-related aging.

Even Skin Tone: Regular use helps create a more uniform, radiant complexion—directly addressing the dullness and uneven texture that often develop during perimenopause and menopause.

Gentle on Sensitive Skin: Unlike harsh brightening ingredients, licorice extract is safe and effective for even the most sensitive menopausal skin, making it ideal for women experiencing increased skin reactivity.

Find Licorice Extract in Empress Naturals' PM Serum.

Organic Prickly Pear Oil

The "Moroccan Gold," also known as Prickly Pear Seed Oil, shields your skin from the various aging factors that sneak up on it. By enhancing collagen formation, this miraculous oil restores your skin's elasticity while acting as an excellent moisturizer.

The oil helps to even out the skin tone and gradually reduces stubborn fine lines and wrinkles.

posts.title: Have You Started Using Serums in Your Skincare?
posts.excerpt: Ultimate protection and care are a must, not a want, for aging menopausal skin. The appearance of wrinkles, flaky skin, hyperpigmentation, and dryness might seem...
posts.content:
Having a skincare routine that takes forever to complete is the last thing anyone wants at the end of a long day. But what if you had the option to incorporate multiple active and concentrated ingredients with a minimalistic routine that only takes a couple of minutes?

With the right natural and organic serums, your skin will get the maximum benefit to look and feel healthy from within.

Do Serums Clog Pores?

It's a total MYTH that using natural oils to your skin will block pores, lead to breakouts, or rashes. Natural oils are really some of the greatest skincare products you can use on your skin to feed, moisturize, and protect it.

Ultimate protection and care are a must, not a want, for aging menopausal skin. The appearance of wrinkles, flaky skin, hyperpigmentation, and dryness might seem unstoppable, but using the appropriate products can turn your skin from dull to radiant without any negative side effects.

How to Pick the Right Serum for Your Skin?

The easiest way to pick a serum suited to your skin is to identify the issues with your skin and look for ingredients that help solve them.

Step 1: Know your Skin Type

Finding your skin type is actually a very simple process. To start, cleanse your face with a gentle cleanser. Wait 30 minutes without applying any product on your skin. If your skin feels tight and dry throughout, you are likely to have dry skin. On the other hand, if it appears glossy or shiny, you have oily skin. If your T-zone is the sole area that shines, you most likely have combination skin. You probably have normal skin if it feels moisturized, pleasant, and non-oily.

If you are looking for some tips on perfecting your routine based on your skin type, make sure you check out our blog on Perimenopausal Skin.

Step 2: Identify Your Skin Needs

After identifying your skin type, you have already eliminated a majority of products that are not suited for it. Now, it is time to identify what menopause skin issues you want to tackle. Do you want to get rid of your hyperpigmentation or reduce fine lines?

Understanding your skin’s needs will help you identify the right ingredients you need to use on your skin.

Step 3: Pick the Right Ingredients

Your skin will tell you what it needs. A myriad of ingredients will help battle different skin issues. For instance, Vitamin A and C in Rosehip Seed Oil will help you get rid of hyperpigmentation and fight visible signs of aging. Licorice Extract in the PM Serum Luxe Night Elixir helps get rid of menopausal hyperpigmentation.

Find a comprehensive list of the best natural ingredients for all your anti-aging needs here.

Serum FAQ’s

  1. What are the benefits of serum for the skin?

Active compounds are concentrated highly in serum. A serum with antioxidants in it will shield the skin from oxidative stress brought on by free radicals. Antioxidants like vitamins C and E are frequently utilized to enhance firmness and texture.

  1. Can we apply face serum every day?

Yes. The majority of facial serums made with natural ingredients are safe to use twice a day. Twice daily, once before bedtime and once early morning on cleansed skin.

Rosehip Seed Oil has been lauded as a miracle worker when it comes to anti-aging skincare. Being Empress Naturals ‘Hero Ingredient’, we use Rosehip Seed Oil in both our bestsellers and are often asked questions about the potency and benefits of this luxurious ingredient.

The oil’s incredible tales date back to ancient times when Egyptians and Mayans used it for its natural skin healing properties. Its multi-purpose use for your hands, neck, hair, and face, combined with its innate beneficial properties, rightfully resulted in Rosehip Seed Oil being titled “Liquid Gold” amongst facial oils.

If you are looking to develop a clean skincare routine, you cannot leave Rosehip Seed Oil out of your list of MUST-HAVES.

This blog will answer all your questions pertaining to Rosehip Seed Oil so you can make the correct choice for your skin.



What is Rosehip Seed Oil, and how is it derived?

Rosehip Seed Oil is a derivative of the fruit of the rosebush plant. The seeds of the plant are pressed to process rosehip seed oil. Due to its thin consistency and excellent skin-absorbing qualities, this oil can be used as a carrier oil that can be successfully combined and utilized with other products or potent essential oils.

Is there a difference between Rosehip Seed Oil and Rosehip Oil?

While these terms are often used interchangeably, there is a significant difference between them. The difference lies in which part of the fruit the oil has been extracted from.

Rosehip Oil is extracted from the entire fruit, which means it contains all the properties of the plant. Rosehip Seed Oil, on the other hand, is oil extracted purely from the seed of the fruit.

Each part of the plant has different benefits for the skin. The seed of the fruit is rich in fatty acids essential to the skin, while the pulp is rich in Vitamin A.

Empress Naturals’ Anti-Aging Skincare Range contains 100% Pure Rosehip Seed Oil that will help you achieve that luminous, youthful complexion. Our Rosehip Seed Oil comes straight from the Southern Alps in South America from ethical and sustainable female-led farms.

Does Rosehip Seed Oil clog pores or leave the skin feeling ‘oily’?

It is a common misconception that all facial oils clog your pores. Rosehip Seed Oil is a ‘Dry Oil’ that has a minimum comedogenic rating of 1. As a Dry Oil, Rosehip Seed Oil seeps straight into your skin and moisturizes it from within, leaving NO greasy or oily feeling behind. This makes Rosehip Seed Oil a great addition to the skincare routine of people who have sensitive or acne-prone skin.

If you are above the age of 40 and looking to make the switch to a clean and conscious skincare routine, make sure you read our blog on Essential Natural Skincare for Women above 40.

How can you use Rosehip Seed Oil on your face?

Rosehip Seed Oil is a multi-purpose product that can be used in ways that suit your preferences and needs. It can be applied as a natural moisturizer or used as a carrier oil for essential oils. You can also mix it with other carrier oils to make a serum or mix it into other moisturizers or lotions.

What are the Benefits of Rosehip Seed Oil for your Skin?

There are numerous benefits of Rosehip Seed Oil for your skin and face.

  • It hydrates your skin from within and leaves it looking healthy and glowing.
  • As a natural exfoliant, Rosehip Seed Oil helps reduce the dullness of your skin.
  • It is a natural protector from sun damage.
  • It promotes collagen production and enhances your skin’s elasticity.
  • It repairs and heals your skin because of its vitamin-rich properties.
  • The essential fatty acids in the oil help reduce hyperpigmentation, reduce scars, and fine lines.
  • Rosehip Seed Oil reduces inflammation and acne.

Choosing any skincare product can be intimidating. You might often find yourself wondering if a product is safe for pregnancy or will help you combat dry skin?

Well, that's the beauty about organic skincare. One thing you never have to be worried about is any harmful effects it may cause to your skin or body.

The real challenge arises when you have to choose the right organic skincare in a market that is filled with greenwashing. If you are looking for some helpful tips on picking the right products for your skin, you are in the right place!

Know Your Skin Type

Whether your skin is dry, sensitive, or oily, organic skincare works to help better its natural health. Knowing your skin type is the first priority before you buy any skin care products. It might be challenging to select the best skin care products based on your skin type. But there are several simple methods to limit your options.

Identifying whether you have dry, oily, or combination skin is a good place to start. Find a product that is hypoallergenic and fragrance-free when you have sensitive skin or are likely to experience irritation.

For those with mature skin, finding the right combination of natural anti-agers can be god sent! Consider the kinds of natural ingredients, including plant extracts and essential oils, that you are looking for in your daily skincare routine.

Read Ingredient Labels

The simplest way to choose the right organic products for your skin is to read product and ingredient labels. Brands that offer pure and organic skincare, often make it a point for their customers to know exactly what is in their products, what its benefits are, and who it is made for.

Products that have been certified as organic are made from plants and other naturally occurring resources. The production of organic products also doesn't include the use of any chemicals, fertilizers, pesticides, or additives.

By doing this, you may be certain that any organic products you use on your skin are genuine, safe, and made organically.

Some Ingredients to Avoid

Parabens

Parabens are preservatives often added to products to keep them bacteria and mold free. While this may seem good for the product, parabens have been found to affect your hormones and cause long term health issues.

You can read more about Parabens here.

Sulfates

Sulfates are cleansers and the worst part about them is that they are incredible at their job. They’re known to strip your hair and scalp of moisture, leaving it dry and brittle.

Read more about Sulfates here.

Phthalates

Phthalates are often used in skincare to keep ingredients flexible till use and prolong the stability of fragrances. They can cause irritation to sensitive skin and strip your skin of natural oils which can be especially harmful to those with dry skin.

posts.content:
The Complete Skincare Guide for Perimenopausal Skin

Women are often told to expect dramatic changes during menopause, but what many don't realize is that these changes begin years earlier during perimenopause. This often-overlooked transition phase brings about hormonal fluctuations that affect your menstrual cycle, body, and, in particular, your skin.

The challenge? There's no one-size-fits-all approach to perimenopausal skincare. If you're wondering how to adapt your routine to combat these changes effectively, you're in the right place. This guide will help you understand the difference between perimenopause and menopause, and show you how to give your skin the extra care it needs during this transition.


What's the Difference: Perimenopause vs Menopause?

Many women aren't sure which phase they're experiencing or how to tell them apart. While both stages share similar symptoms, understanding the key differences is essential for creating an effective skincare routine.

What is Menopause?

Menopause officially begins after you've gone 12 consecutive months without a menstrual period. While it can occur earlier or later, most women experience natural menopause between ages 40 and 58, with the median age being 51 years.

What is Perimenopause?

Perimenopause is the transitional phase when your menstrual cycle begins to fluctuate. This phase typically starts in your late 30s or early 40s and lasts between 2 to 8 years on average. During this time, your hormone levels shift unpredictably as your ovaries gradually produce less estrogen.

The symptoms of perimenopause mirror those of menopause in many ways. You might

  • feel tired
  • get hot flashes
  • feel sore in your breasts
  • lose your sex drive
  • experience vaginal dryness
  • have difficulties falling asleep
  • experience mood swings

The key difference: During perimenopause, your periods become irregular but don't stop completely, and pregnancy is still possible. Once you've reached menopause, menstruation ceases entirely.


How Does Your Skin Change during Perimenopause?

Numerous hormonal changes that occur during perimenopause might have an impact on your skin. Research shows that declining estrogen levels trigger a cascade of skin changes, including decreased collagen production, reduced elasticity, and increased moisture loss. This condition, known as Estrogen Deficient Skin, is the primary driver of skin issues during this transition.

What Happens to Your Skin

Increased Dryness and Sensitivity
Estrogen plays a crucial role in maintaining your skin's moisture barrier and supporting collagen production. As estrogen levels drop, your skin loses its ability to retain water effectively, leading to persistent dryness and increased sensitivity.

Accelerated Aging Signs
Studies indicate that women lose up to 30% of their skin collagen in the first five years of menopause. This rapid decline results in:

  • More pronounced fine lines and wrinkles, especially around the eyes and mouth
  • Loss of skin firmness and elasticity
  • Sagging, particularly around the neck, jawline, and cheeks
  • Thinner skin that bruises more easily

Hyperpigmentation and Uneven Tone
Age spots and hyperpigmentation become more visible as skin thins. The cell renewal process slows down significantly, meaning dark spots and scars take longer to fade.

Adult Acne Breakouts
Surprisingly, up to 30% of perimenopausal women experience new or worsening acne. The fluctuating hormone levels can trigger increased oil production and inflammation.


1. Combat Dryness with Gentle, Nourishing Products

A. Layer on Hydration
Hydration is non-negotiable during perimenopause and menopause. Focus on natural, plant-based ingredients that work with your skin:

  • Rosehip Seed Oil: This botanical powerhouse is naturally rich in essential fatty acids, vitamin A, and vitamin C. Its emollient properties help improve moisture retention, support skin regeneration, and boost collagen production. Empress Naturals offers cold-pressed 100% Pure and USDA Certified Organic Rosehip Seed Oil. Known as "Liquid Gold" among facial oils, this odorless, anhydrous dry oil instantly absorbs into your skin, hydrating from within and leaving behind soft, smooth skin
  • Centella Asiatica (Cica): This healing herb has been used for centuries to soothe, hydrate, and repair skin. Cica strengthens the skin barrier, reduces inflammation, and promotes moisture retention naturally. It's particularly effective at boosting collagen production, which is crucial during perimenopause when collagen loss accelerates.


2. Address Sensitivity and Inflammation

A. Embrace Vitamin C
Vitamin C is essential for collagen production and helps protect and heal sensitive skin. Research shows it can improve skin texture, reduce redness, and brighten hyperpigmentation. Natural sources like rosehip seed oil contain bioavailable vitamin C that's gentler on sensitive skin. Empress Naturals' entire Anti-Aging Skincare Range features cold-pressed 100% Pure and USDA Certified Organic Rosehip Seed Oil as a hero ingredient, delivering vitamin C and vitamin A naturally to your skin.

B. Try Anti-Inflammatory Ingredients
Look for products containing:

  • Centella Asiatica (Cica): This ancient healing herb soothes irritation, reduces redness, and supports skin healing while strengthening the moisture barrier
  • Natural Plant Oils: Rosehip seed oil, argan oil, and geranium oil are rich in antioxidants and have powerful anti-inflammatory properties that accelerate cell renewal and calm sensitive skin
  • Calendula: Gentle and soothing, calendula reduces inflammation and promotes skin healing naturally.

3. Fight Hyperpigmentation and Dark Spots

A. Try Targeted Natural Brightening Ingredients
Look for botanical alternatives that gently brighten without harsh chemicals:

  • Vitamin C-Rich Oils: Rosehip seed oil and sea buckthorn oil naturally contain high levels of vitamin C for brightening
  • Licorice Root Extract: Natural brightening agent with anti-inflammatory properties that works overnight to reduce inflammation and fade pigmentation, especially dark spots brought on by hormonal shifts

posts.content:
We all know it is coming but no one can prepare you for the intense changes menopause brings in the texture, structure, and appearance of your skin. Your skin has gone through a lot by the time you approach menopause, nearly five decades of unpredictable weather, acne, makeup, moisturiser, smiles, and tears.

It's not a surprise that as you get closer to menopause, your skin won't likely resemble your youthful skin's dewy appearance. But, let's be honest: your youthful skin probably wasn't entirely flawless either. We all deal with skin issues throughout our lives and why should menopause be any different?

The good news here is that it is truly not very difficult to combat the signs of aging that happen to your face once you understand what your skin’s needs are!

A clean, minimalistic, and effective routine that targets your skin concerns is ALL you need!


How Does Your Skin Change During Menopause?

As you become older, your skin often changes as a result of two things: extrinsic, or intrinsic factors. Extrinsic factors include pollution, stress, and sun exposure that can cause your skin to age.

Contrarily, intrinsic factors that cause skin to age over time include the depletion of collagen. Collagen is the protein that gives our skin its strength and flexibility and hence a loss of it through a long period of time can cause our skin to look and feel older.

As your skin changes during perimenopause and menopause, your body declines the production of estrogen in your ovaries, but the production of androgens i.e. male sex hormones like testosterone, remains the same.

Estrogen drives the creation of both collagen and elastin in our bodies. These proteins are required to make your skin hydrated and healthy. Hence, this sudden imbalance results in dry skin on your face, body, and everywhere else.

While these processes are not fully reversible, there is a lot that can be done to help you revive your glow using the right ingredients, products, and routines. The easiest method to handle the outward signs of menopause is to switch to organic and clean skincare that is designed for women with aged skin.

If you want to know more about how your skin ages and general tips you can follow to keep it healthy, read our blog on achieving ageless skin.

Here are some changes in your skin that you might notice and how you can improve them:

Sagging Skin, Fine Lines, and Wrinkles

A common sign of aging, wrinkles, sagging skin, and fine lines, is a direct result of losing collagen in your skin. It is said that women lose about 30% of their collagen in the first five years of menopause.

What can you do?

  • Incorporate Vitamin A in your skincare:
    Rosehip Seed Oil is nature’s Vitamin A and the answer to all your skin’s anti-aging woes. Rosehip Seed Oil increases your cell turnover, making your skin smooth and texture-free while minimizing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.

    Empress Naturals’     Anti-Aging Skincare Range with 100% Pure and Organic Rosehip Seed Oil is all you need for the perfect minimalistic routine.

Dull and Dry Skin:

Menopause-related sudden skin changes can come as an unpleasant shock. Nobody wants their skin to be dry, dull, and irritated, after all. As described earlier, the amount of estrogen in your blood lowers as you age, and the collagen formation in your skin slows.

What can you do?

  • Opt for gentle cleansers : Choose gentle cleansers instead of abrasive ones to avoid drying your skin out. Make sure to use a mild, non-foaming cleanser that is creamy in texture and leaves your skin feeling hydrated.

  • Up your moisturizing game: It's important to use moisturizers that give your skin its moisture back. Choose products with clean, sustainable and environmentally friendly ingredients, like the Empress Naturals AM Serum that moisturizes your skin from within and leave no greasy residue.

If you want to know more about how you can combat itchy and dry skin cause due to menopause, make sure you check out our blog.

Hyperpigmentation and Dark Spots:

With thinning skin, the appearance of dark spots and hyperpigmentation tends to get elaborated. Scars and spots might take longer to disappear because of delayed cell renewal.

What can you do?

  • Target the root cause: To avoid hyperpigmentation from occurring, visit your dermatologist and try to understand why it occurs. Targeting the root cause of your hyperpigmentation will save you a lot of time, money, and effort. Using ingredients that specifically target hyperpigmentation, like licorice extract in the Empress Naturals Luxe Night Elixir will improve your chances of targeting dark spots at the root.

Breakouts and Adult Acne:

Due to the increased prevalence of androgens in comparison to estrogen, pimples and blemishes are frequently a typical occurrence. Our skin's ability to replace old tissues and regenerate new ones depletes as we become older. The combination of illness and congested pores is optimal.

What can you do?

  • Add mild anti-inflammatories to your skincare regimen: Rosehip Seed Oil, Argan Oil, Geranium Oil, etc are abundant in antioxidants, work as anti-inflammatory and rejuvenating agents to speed up the cell turnover process in your skin.

Try Empress Naturals Luxe Night Elixir, which is designed for all skin types and has the ideal anti-inflammatory ingredients!

posts.excerpt: You’ve reached our skincare sanctuary, where the journey to radiant and well-nourished skin begins! In this blog, we'll embark on a transformative expedition through the...
posts.content:
You’ve reached our skincare sanctuary, where the journey to radiant and well-nourished skin begins! In this blog, we'll embark on a transformative expedition through the world of skincare layering, a time-tested technique that can take your hydration game to unprecedented heights. Are you ready to unlock the secrets to a dewy and luminous complexion?

Healthy, hydrated skin is not only a reflection of our inner well-being but also a testament to our dedication to self-care. However, with the myriad of skincare products flooding the market, knowing where to start can be overwhelming. Fear not! By adopting the art of layering, we'll delve into a step-by-step approach that optimizes the benefits of each product, culminating in a deeply nourished and plump skin texture.

Throughout this journey, we'll explore the fundamental principles of skincare layering, understanding the importance of using the right products in the right order. From the gentle cleansers that whisk away impurities to the revitalizing essences that prime your skin for absorption, we'll delve into the science behind each step.

If you are looking to add hydration to your skincare routine, we've got you covered! From CICA to Squalane, read all about the hydrating ingredients for your skin.

Layer Your Skincare Correctly

Step 1: Cleanse and Exfoliate - The Foundation of Hydration

Achieving maximum hydration starts with a clean canvas. Begin by selecting a gentle cleanser that suits your skin type, effectively removing dirt, excess oil, and impurities. Cleansing prepares your skin for optimal hydration by clearing away any barriers that may prevent products from absorbing properly.


 Exfoliation, whether physical or chemical, is a vital step to incorporate into your routine. By gently sloughing off dead skin cells, you reveal a fresh and smooth surface for enhanced product absorption.

This allows subsequent layers of skincare to penetrate deeply, maximizing their hydrating benefits. Choose exfoliators tailored to your skin's sensitivity and follow the recommended usage frequency to avoid over-exfoliation, which can lead to dryness and irritation.


Step 2: Tone and Balance - Preparing the Skin's pH

Toning is an underrated yet essential step that sets the stage for effective hydration. A well-formulated toner helps restore the skin's pH balance, calm irritation, and prepare it for the subsequent layers of skincare.

Look for toners infused with hydrating ingredients like rose water, which provide an extra boost of moisture to your skin. Applying toner after cleansing helps to lock in hydration and create an optimal environment for the next steps in your skincare routine.


Step 3: Hydration Powerhouses - Serums and Essences

Now that your skin is clean and primed, it's time to introduce the heroes of hydration: serums and essences. These concentrated formulas contain potent active ingredients that address specific skin concerns and deliver intense hydration.

When selecting serums and essences, choose products that target your particular needs, whether it's dryness, fine lines, or dullness. Apply these products in order of thinnest to thickest consistency, allowing each layer to absorb fully before moving on to the next.

This layering technique ensures maximum penetration of the active ingredients, providing deep nourishment and hydration.

Read our blog on the Top 5 Hydration Heroes to know which ingredients will help maximize your skin’s hydration levels.


Step 4: Lock in Moisture - The Importance of Moisturizers

Moisturizers are the ultimate sealants that lock in all the hydration and goodness from the previous skincare steps. Choose a moisturizer suited to your skin type, whether it's a lightweight gel, a creamy lotion, or a rich balm.

Look for ingredients like ceramides, hyaluronic acid, or plant oils that replenish moisture and strengthen the skin's barrier function. Applying moisturizer as the final step in your routine helps create a protective barrier that prevents moisture loss throughout the day.

It seals in all the hydrating layers beneath, ensuring that your skin remains supple, nourished, and hydrated.

Read our blog on How to pick the Right Moisturiser.


Step 5: Shield and Protect - Don't Forget Sunscreen

No skincare routine is complete without the crucial step of sun protection. Applying a broad-spectrum sunscreen with an SPF of 30 or higher is essential to shield your skin from harmful UV rays.

Sunscreen not only prevents sunburn and premature aging but also helps maintain the hydration levels you've worked so hard to achieve through layering skincare.

Apply a generous amount of sunscreen to all exposed areas of your face and body, including the neck, ears, and hands. Reapply every two hours, or more frequently if sweating or swimming. By incorporating sunscreen into your layering routine, you ensure that your skin remains protected, healthy, and hydrated.


Step 6: Use a Mask - Once in a while!

Facial masks are a powerful tool for hydrating the skin. These masks contain highly concentrated ingredients that deeply penetrate the skin, providing an extra dose of moisture. The key benefits of using a facial mask for hydration include:

  • Intense Hydration: Facial masks have a higher concentration of hydrating ingredients like hyaluronic acid or aloe vera, which attract and retain moisture in the skin. They deliver a surge of hydration, leaving the skin plump and nourished.
  • Enhanced Absorption: The occlusive nature of masks creates a barrier, allowing the active ingredients to penetrate deeply. This prolonged contact time ensures better absorption, reaching the deeper layers of the skin for maximum hydration.
  • Skin Barrier Support: Many masks contain ingredients that strengthen the skin's barrier function, preventing water loss and protecting against dehydration.

Some Tips to Stay Hydrated, Always!

Achieving and maintaining hydrated skin goes beyond your skincare routine alone. If you're seeking to achieve maximum hydration, here are some tips you have to follow

Tip #1: Avoid Excessive Exfoliation

When it comes to hydration, remember not to over-exfoliate. We advise against using multiple exfoliating products like cleansers, toners, and serums.

Over-exfoliation can lead to redness, dryness, irritation, and may exacerbate conditions like rosacea, perioral dermatitis, and acne.


Tip #2: Pay Attention to Your Body, Hands, and Lips

Don't neglect your hands and lips in your pursuit of replenished skin. Apply a nourishing ointment throughout the day and before bed to keep your hands and lips moisturized, allowing the rest of your body to benefit from its replenishing properties.

Our Calming Massage Oil is the ideal remedy for hydration. Enriched with high-quality essential oils, this unique blend not only nourishes and moisturizes your skin, but also promotes a restful sleep experience, revitalizing you in numerous ways.


Tip #3: Enhance Bedroom Moisture with a Humidifier

An effective way to combat dryness and prevent overnight dehydration of your skin is to incorporate a humidifier in your bedroom.

By increasing the moisture levels in the air, a humidifier helps maintain optimal humidity, resulting in healthier-looking skin that remains balanced and hydrated.

Adding a humidifier to your bedroom is a simple yet powerful step towards ensuring your skin receives the necessary moisture it needs to thrive.

What are Essential Oils? What is their Role in Massage?


Essential oils are essentially (see what we did there!) concentrated oil formulations that have the benefits of natural ingredients.

They have multiple uses in the self-care and wellness space because of their multi-tasking abilities powered by nature’s healing properties.

A massage that uses essential oils is often also known as an aromatherapy massage. Aromatherapy massage combines the healing properties of essential oils and massage therapy to aid the rejuvenation and wellness of both, your mind and body.

Aromatherapy massages introduce aromatic essential oils to the skin. However, they are never used directly on the skin without being diluted. 2-5% solutions of essential oils with a carrier oil like jojoba oil or sweet almond are most popularly used, doubling the benefits of any aromatherapy massage.

As essential oils are absorbed by your skin, they enter your body. Depending on the oils used, these essential oils provide advantages to both your mind and body, such as enhancing your mood, relieving discomfort, and detoxifying your body.

Empress Tip

Essential Oils are the best multi-taskers: Essential oils are also commonly diffused during aromatherapy massages.

Add 3-4 drops of your favorite essential oil scent to your diffuser and let the aroma fill up the room! Make sure to pick a pairing similar to oil in your massage!

Inhaling the scent of essential oils will trigger your limbic system and regulate emotions. This indicates that the aromas you are exposed to can directly affect your emotions, uplifting or relaxing them as desired!

Benefits of Essential Oils in Massage


Aromatherapy massage combines the therapeutic effects of essential oils with massage therapy.

Aromatherapy massage has the ability to affect all of your body's systems, from the cardiovascular and neurological systems to lymphatic drainage and musculoskeletal systems. Not only is it an extremely soothing and restorative process but rejuvenating in the best sense!

Here are some of the best benefits we have witnessed with essential oils in massages. If you want a breakdown of each oil and it’s benefits, make sure to check out our Guide on Massage Oil Formulations

DIY Essential Oils formulation for Better Emotional Well-being and Calm

Your limbic system is also in charge of controlling and regulating your emotions. Because of this interrelated relationship, aromatherapy can readily tap into your moods and impact your psychological health.

During an aromatherapy massage, inhaling essential oils like lavender or chamomile can help to settle your mind and feelings, promoting rest and helping you sleep better!

A 2017 research looked at the effects of aromatherapy treatment on nurses working schedules. The aromatherapy massage group was seen to show better sleep quality and less sleep disruption.

posts.title: Amazing facts about Rosehip seed oil
posts.excerpt: Beauty is only skin-deep – the popular saying goes pointing on the innate beauty of character. You may think it’s the ultimate truth and...
posts.content:
Beauty is only skin-deep – the popular saying goes pointing on the innate beauty of character. You may think it’s the ultimate truth and may choose to go with the flow – busy, drifting towards where life takes you.

Of course, inner beauty is significant, but that doesn’t mean that what you are externally doesn’t count!

When a barrage of age signs intrudes into your skin, you can’t help but look bewildered! It’s high time to spruce up your old skin care regimen to save it from further damage. Give a glow-up time for your skin, too.

Rosehip seed oil is an amazing element incorporated into some of the most subtle skin-enhancing products because of its varied skin-healing properties. It’s a powerhouse of skin-friendly components.

Let’s know more about Rosehip seed oil. What is Rosehip seed oil?

This ‘Queen of all beauty oils’ is extracted from the seeds of the Rosa rubiginosa, seen in the southern regions of the Andes, mostly found in Chile or from Rosa canina, a wild rose species seen spread over Europe, western Asia, and the north-western sides of Africa.

Rose hip seed oil is pressed from the seeds of the rose plant. This oil can be used as a carrier oil, which can be effectively mixed and used with other products or intense essential oils due to its thin consistency and high skin-absorbing properties.

Rosehip seed oil penetrates your skin layers to balance the oil production, minimizing it, which in turn unclogs the pores. The appearances of age signs, blemishes, even stretch marks and scars look diminished.

Benefits of Rose hip seed oil for your skin

Rosehip seed oil brings a natural glow and even tone to the skin. It plays a vital role in fighting the age signs, boosting collagen production.

Keeps dry skin moisturized and supple

Being a non-greasy oil, it helps retain the moisture of the skin.

The essential fatty acids in the oil, which include linoleic (omega-6) and linolenic acid (omega-3), help to keep the cell walls strong enough to maintain the water balance of the skin.

Exfoliates the skin

Rosehip seed oil is a natural exfoliant. With ample amounts of Vitamin A, the oil helps reduce dryness and dullness in your skin, making it glowing and lively.

Repairs and heals the skin

Loaded with vitamins A, K, B1, B2, and B3, this oil has amazing healing and skin-repairing properties. The damage caused to the skin can be visibly reduced by the regular use of this oil.

Protection from sun damage

Ravages caused by constant UV exposure to the sun plays a pivotal role in premature aging and inhibit natural collagen production.

Rosehip oil contains antioxidants like vitamins A, C, and E, which synergistically fight visible sun damage and prevent photoaging. But as this can’t be used directly as a sunscreen, you need to consult your dermatologist before replacing this with your sunblock.

Skin regeneration, reduction of scars, and fine lines

The essential fatty acids and antioxidants in rosehip oil play a vital role in tissue and cell regeneration of the skin.

The oil is proven to be effective for skin healing, reduction of scars, fine lines, wrinkles, and crow’s feet.

Boosts skin elasticity and collagen production

This is the go-to oil for severely aged-looking skin. Rich in Vitamins A and C, necessary elements for the production of collagen, the oil significantly improves collagen production, thereby bringing back skin’s elasticity and suppleness.

Reduces skin inflammation

With good amounts of polyphenols and anthocyanins, the inflammation-reducing components, along with Vitamin E, an antioxidant with its anti-inflammatory effects, rosehip seed oil reduces skin inflammation and skin irritation considerably. Brightens and evens out the skin: Enriched with Vitamin C, the oil visibly brightens up your skin, making it vibrant and refreshed.

Reduces hyperpigmentation

The Vitamin A in rose hip seed oil is a rich source of several nutritional compounds like retinoids, which help reduce hyperpigmentation and other visible age signs of skin when used regularly

Reduces acne

Rosehip seed oil is known for its anti-microbial properties and low comedogenicity. It doesn’t clog the skin pores, thus reduces the menacing acne formation.

How to use Rosehip seed oil on your skin?

This oil is non-greasy and dry, and it gets quickly absorbed into the skin. The oil is usually safe for all types of skin, but it is advisable to go for a patch test to ensure that the oil doesn’t cause any allergies to your skin.

How to do the patch test:

  • Apply a little amount of the oil to your wrist
  • Cover the area with a gauze or a band-aid
  • Check after 24 hours for signs of irritation or inflammation
  • If your skin is irritated, itchy, or inflamed, you should not use rosehip seed oil. Consult your dermatologist if the irritation persists.
  • If there are no signs of irritation, the oil is safe to use.
  • After a successful patch test, you can use rosehip seed on your skin up to twice daily.
  • The oil can be applied on its own, used with your regular moisturizer or mixed with other potent essential oils for long-lasting effects.

UV rays can lessen the potency or make the rosehip seed oil rancid if exposed. So, a dark colored bottle is ideal to keep the product and has to be stored in a cool, dark place for extended shelf life.

Importance of cold-pressed, organic Rosehip seed oil for skin

Pure, organic cold-pressed Rosehip seed oil has long-lasting and outstanding effects on the skin. While using the cold-pressed technique, the oil is extracted from the seed, nut, and kernel of the plant at low temperatures.

This helps to prevent the decomposition of thermosensitive compounds in the oil, making it literally organic to safeguard your skin in the most natural way.

It’s imperative to choose clean beauty products that are 100% natural, organic with transparent labelling of all the ingredients used in their product development. Never choose low-quality or low-cost products that are compromised.

Your skin is unique and keeping it youthful, supple and radiant is an art. Read more about how to keep your skin healthy and youthful on our blogs.

Nourish, Moisturise and Protect Mature Skin with Natural Ingredients

Do you avoid using facial oils in your skincare routine because you’re worried about clogged pores? If yes, then you need to change that right away!

The notion that using natural oils on your skin can clog pores, cause blemishes, or rashes is a complete MYTH! In fact, natural oils are amongst the best skincare products you can apply to your skin that nourish, moisturise, and protect your skin.

With menopausal skin, ultimate care and protection are a necessity, not a want. The dryness, hyperpigmentation, flaky skin, and wrinkles can seem relentless, but the right products will help you go from dull to glowing skin without any side effects. In this blog, we are highlighting some of the best facial oils for mature and menopausal skin. If you want healthy and radiant skin, add them to your cart and wait for the magic to arrive!

Why Menopausal Skin Needs Specialized Anti-Aging Care

During menopause, hormonal changes trigger multiple skin concerns simultaneously:

  • Collagen depletion leading to fine lines and wrinkles
  • Decreased sebum production causes severe dryness
  • Slower cell turnover results in dull, uneven skin tone
  • Increased sensitivity to environmental stressors
  • Hyperpigmentation and age spots

The best skincare for menopausal skin must address all these concerns while providing deep nourishment without irritation. Natural facial oils offer the perfect solution, delivering concentrated nutrients that synthetic ingredients simply cannot match.

The 5 Best Face Oils for Menopausal Skin: Your Anti-Aging Arsenal

1. Rosehip Seed Oil

Rosehip Seed Oil has been used for millennia by women as an anti-aging oil because of its abundance of benefits for the skin. Rosehip oil contains fatty acids, including linoleic acid and oleic acid, as well as vitamins A and C.

Research has found Rosehip Seed Oil to have high antioxidant capabilities that can help rejuvenate menopausal skin. According to studies, they guard against UV-induced free radical damage, encourage collagen production, and assist with wrinkle reduction. Vitamin A found in rosehip oil also helps your skin by restoring UV damage, reducing wrinkles, treating inflammation and hyperpigmentation.


Empress Naturals’ Anti-Aging Face Serum uses 100% pure, undiluted, and cold-pressed Rosehip Seed Oil.

If you want to read more about this multipurpose ingredient, make sure you read our blog on Rosehip Seed Oil. A must-have to include in any anti-aging skincare routine, as it nourishes your skin from within and has zero harmful chemicals.

2. Organic Argan Oil

Argan Oil (Argania Spinosa) delivers comprehensive anti-aging benefits that make it one of the best facial oils for aging skin:

  • Anti-Aging: Argan Oil increases collagen formation, enhancing skin suppleness.
  • Sun Damage Protection: Argan oil's antioxidant compounds assist in defending the skin against the free radical damage caused by the sun, hence preventing skin ageing and skin cancer.
  • Moisturising: The high vitamin E content of argan oil enhances your skin's capacity to retain water, nourishing it from the inside.
  • Combats Skin Inflammation: Argan Oil fights inflammation by acting as an antibacterial agent and reducing skin redness. It is the perfect addition to a skincare routine for those with mature and sensitive skin.

3. Organic Jojoba Oil

Jojoba Oil (Simmondsia Chinensis) is technically a wax ester that closely mimics your skin's natural sebum, making it ideal for menopausal skin that's lost its natural oil production

  • Superior Hydration: Creates a protective barrier that prevents water loss
  • Non-Comedogenic: Won't clog pores despite being deeply moisturizing
  • Vitamin E Rich: Provides antioxidant protection against aging
  • Balancing: Regulates oil production for combination menopausal skin

4. Organic Geranium Oil

A plant species native to South Africa, Pelargonium Graveolens, yields geranium essential oil.

Geranium essential oil has been proven to have antimicrobial, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties that are effective in reducing acne blemishes, itchiness, flaky skin, and skin infections when applied topically. High in anti-inflammatory properties, geranium essential oil is also beneficial for a number of inflammatory illnesses like skin discolouration and redness, leaving your skin calm and moisturised.

5. Natural Squalane Oil

Squalane oil is high in antioxidants that help fight free radicals and damage to the skin that causes ageing. Additionally, as a detoxifier, squalane can boost collagen production, giving your skin a smooth texture.

Despite being an oil, it is extremely lightweight and noncomedogenic, so it will not clog your pores. Squalane also has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness. All in all, it is the perfect moisturising oil that penetrates the pores and improves the skin at the molecular level rather than leaving the skin feeling sluggish.

The Science Behind Essential Oils for Menopause

Research consistently shows that the best natural skincare for menopause includes oils rich in:

  • Phytoestrogens: Plant compounds that may help compensate for declining estrogen
  • Ceramides: Essential for maintaining skin barrier function
  • Antioxidants: Crucial for preventing and reversing oxidative damage
  • Essential Fatty Acids: Vital for skin cell membrane health

These components work synergistically to address the root causes of menopausal skin aging, not just the symptoms.

posts.content:
As women enter their 40s, hormonal changes—particularly during perimenopause and menopause—dramatically impact skin health. The decline in estrogen levels accelerates collagen breakdown, reduces skin elasticity, and decreases natural oil production. This comprehensive guide will help you build an effective anti-aging skin care routine for the 40s that addresses these unique challenges with safe, non-toxic anti-aging skin care solutions.

Why Your 40s Require a Different Skincare Approach

The Science Behind Aging Skin in Your 40s

When you reach your 40s, your skin undergoes significant changes:

  • Collagen production decreases by 1% annually after age 30, accelerating in your 40s
  • Cell turnover slows down, causing dead skin cells to accumulate
  • Natural oil production diminishes, leading to increased dryness
  • Hormonal fluctuations during menopause further compromise skin barrier function
  • Sun damage from previous decades becomes more visible as dark spots and wrinkles

These changes to perimenopausal and menopausal skin make it crucial to transition to an all-natural skincare routine that supports your skin's changing needs without harsh chemicals that can further compromise your skin's barrier.

Building Your Non-Toxic Anti-Aging Skin Care Routine

Step 1: Gentle Exfoliation (2-3 times weekly)

Dead skin cell buildup is a major concern in your 40s. Regular exfoliation with natural ingredients helps:

  • Remove dulling dead skin cells
  • Improve product absorption
  • Stimulate cellular renewal
  • Reveal brighter, smoother skin

Recommended approach: Use mild, plant-based exfoliators containing natural fruit acids or gentle physical exfoliants like rice powder or oatmeal.

Step 2: Hydrating Anti-Aging Serums

The cornerstone of any effective organic skincare routine is a high-quality anti-aging serum. Look for serums containing:

  • Rosehip seed oil: Rich in vitamins A and C, promotes collagen production
  • CICA: Prevents moisture loss and reinforces the skin barrier
  • Plant-based peptides: Support skin firmness and elasticity

Morning routine: Apply a vitamin C-rich serum to protect against environmental damage and boost collagen synthesis.

Step 3: Deep Moisturization

Mature skin requires more intensive hydration than younger skin. Your all-natural skin care routine should include:

Morning moisturizer: Lightweight, containing natural humectants

  • Rosehip seed oil (Rosa Canina): The gold standard for anti-aging, packed with vitamins A, C, and essential fatty acids that promote collagen synthesis and skin renewal

  • Cranberry seed oil: Rich in omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids, plus natural antioxidants that protect against environmental damage

  • Carrot seed oil: Contains beta-carotene and vitamin A precursors that support cellular regeneration and give skin a healthy glow

  • Centella Asiatica (Cica): A powerful healing botanical that reduces inflammation and supports collagen production

Evening treatment: Richer formulations with nourishing oils

  • Licorice root extract: A natural brightening agent that helps fade dark spots and even skin tone

  • Postbiotic ferment: Supports the skin's microbiome for healthier, more resilient skin

  • Pomegranate oil: Contains rare punicic acid that stimulates collagen production and provides potent antioxidant protection

  • Vegan squalane: A lightweight yet deeply penetrating oil that mimics skin's natural moisture factors and enhances barrier function

Targeting Specific Concerns in Your 40s

Eye Care for Mature Skin

The delicate eye area shows aging signs first. An effective eye treatment should address:

  • Crow's feet and fine lines
  • Dark circles and puffiness
  • Loss of firmness in upper eyelids

Look for eye creams with caffeine, peptides, and antioxidant-rich plant extracts.

Night-Time Skin Renewal

Your organic skincare routine should include a dedicated night treatment that works while you sleep:

  • Rosehip seed oil: Promotes cellular regeneration
  • Natural retinol alternatives: Bakuchiol or hibiscus extract
  • Nourishing botanical oils: Support overnight repair processes

Understanding Hormonal Impact

During perimenopause and menopause, declining estrogen levels cause:

  • Decreased collagen production
  • Reduced skin thickness
  • Increased sensitivity and dryness
  • Slower wound healing

Adapting Your Routine for Hormonal Changes

Your non toxic anti aging skin care routine should evolve with these changes:

  1. Increase hydration: Use multiple layers of hydrating products
  2. Gentle formulations: Avoid harsh actives that can irritate sensitive skin
  3. Barrier support: Focus on ceramides and natural oils
  4. Consistent routine: Maintain regular application for best results

Building an effective all-natural skin care routine in your 40s isn't just about addressing visible signs of aging—it's about supporting your skin's health through hormonal changes and environmental stressors. By choosing non-toxic anti-aging skin care products and maintaining consistent habits, you can achieve radiant, healthy skin that reflects your inner vitality.

Remember, every skin type is unique. Start slowly when introducing new products, and consider consulting with a skincare professional to customize your organic skincare routine for your specific needs.
