posts.content:
Rosehip seed oil for face care has earned its reputation as the 'Queen of Natural Skincare' – and for good reason. This powerful botanical oil has captured the attention of celebrities, dermatologists, and skincare enthusiasts worldwide, making it one of the most sought-after ingredients in the rosehip seed oil market today.

Whether you're battling signs of aging, dealing with hormonal skin changes during menopause, or simply seeking a natural solution for healthier skin, understanding how to choose the right rosehip seed oil for face application can transform your skincare routine.

You can read more about the incredible benefits of Rosehip Seed Oil in our blog on the Amazing Facts about Rosehip Seed Oil.

What Makes Rosehip Seed Oil Perfect for Face Care?

Rosehip seed oil is extracted from Rosa Canina, a wild rose bush native to Chile's Southern Alps regions. This multipurpose oil excels at addressing common facial skin concerns, including premature aging, inflammation, scarring, and uneven texture. When applied to the face, rosehip seed oil penetrates deeply to deliver essential fatty acids and vitamins that promote youthful, luminous skin. The benefits of rosehip seed oil are almost endless.

The wild rose bushes grow in Chile's mountainous regions, where harsh conditions create resilient plants packed with skin-nourishing compounds. After the rose petals fall, the seeds and berries are harvested and processed to create this liquid gold for your face.

Essential Oils for Menopause: Why Rosehip Seed Oil Stands Out

For women experiencing menopause, hormonal changes can dramatically affect skin health. When exploring what essential oils are good for menopause-related skin concerns, rosehip seed oil consistently ranks among the top choices. Its high concentration of vitamin A and essential fatty acids helps combat the dryness, thinning, and loss of elasticity that commonly occur during this life stage.

Unlike traditional essential oils that require dilution, rosehip seed oil for face use is gentle enough to apply directly to skin, making it an ideal choice for mature women seeking effective, natural skincare solutions during menopause.

How to Choose the Best Rosehip Seed Oil for Your Face

Extraction Method: The Foundation of Quality

Like most oils, Rosehip Seed Oil can be extracted in multiple ways from the plant. However, the extraction method used can determine the purity and apt consistency of the final product.

Cold pressing is the best way to extract rosehip oil since it preserves the amazing qualities of the oil. This extraction method creates pure, lightweight rosehip seed oil by cold-pressing the rosehip seeds to extract the oil from them. Information about how a brand extracts their Rosehip Seed Oil should be mentioned on their website and can be the first indicator towards the purity of the product.

Plant Parts Used: Maximizing Facial Benefits

While Rosehip Seed Oil is an incredible addition to the skincare routine for any skin type, it is important for you to determine what your skin needs are. Different parts of the plant are rich in differing nutrients that help your skin. While the seeds of the plant are rich in essential fatty-acids, the pulp and skin have an abundance of Vitamin A.

Hence, choosing a product that is the best of both worlds, i.e. is extracted from both the pulp and the seed is the best bet for your skin.

Quality Indicators: Color, Consistency, and Fragrance

The best quality Rosehip Seed Oils should have the following physical characteristics:

Color

Cold-pressed Rosehip Seed Oil of the finest quality can be any shade from a stunning deep golden tint to a red/orange tone. If the oil looks pale, it may have undergone extensive processing or originated from a subpar source.

Consistency

100% pure Rosehip Seed Oil is light and effortlessly absorbs into the skin. As a Dry Oil it seeps straight into your skin, hydrating it from within. thanks to its consistency. t's consistency should be thick and rightfully resemble 'liquid gold'.

Fragrance

Rosehip Seed Oil's scent is a key factor in determining whether it is truly pure and natural. It smells very differently from roses and has a very distinct, faint woody aroma. If your oil smells like roses or has added fragrances, chances are it contains preservatives and perfumes and is not pure.

Some people may find the smell too strong. For them, we offer deodorised Rosehip seed oil.


Organic Origins Matter

The purest form of Rosehip Seed Oil is extracted from organically grown plants. Ethically and sustainably farmed plants are extremely important in finding the best quality serums or products that have pure Rosehip Seed Oil.

If you have any other questions relating to Rosehip Seed Oil, make sure you check out our blog where we answer questions all things Rosehip Seed Oil.

Rosehip Seed Oil for Face Care During Different Life Stages

For Aging Skin

As we age, facial skin loses collagen and elasticity. Rosehip seed oil for face application helps stimulate cell regeneration and improve skin texture, making it an excellent anti-aging solution.

For Menopausal Skin

When considering what essential oils are good for menopause-related skin changes, rosehip seed oil addresses multiple concerns simultaneously – dryness, sensitivity, and loss of firmness that commonly affect facial skin during this transition.

For Acne-Prone Skin

Despite being an oil, rosehip seed oil for face use won't clog pores. Its anti-inflammatory properties actually help reduce acne and fade post-inflammatory scarring.

Making the Right Choice for Your Face

When selecting rosehip seed oil for face care, prioritize products that are:

  • USDA Certified Organic
  • Cold-pressed
  • Clearly labeled with all ingredients
  • Sourced from reputable suppliers
  • Free from synthetic additives

Your facial skin deserves the purest, most effective treatment possible. By understanding what makes good rosehip oil brands stand out in today's market, you can make an informed decision that supports your skin's health and beauty goals.

Whether you're addressing menopausal skin changes or simply seeking a natural anti-aging solution, the right rosehip seed oil for face care can provide the nourishment and protection your skin needs to maintain its youthful radiance for years to come

posts.title: Safe Natural Skincare During Pregnancy: What to Use & Avoid
posts.excerpt: Pregnancy brings significant changes to a woman's body, including how her skin behaves and reacts to products. Many expectant mothers become more conscious about what...
posts.content:
Pregnancy brings significant changes to a woman's body, including how her skin behaves and reacts to products. Many expectant mothers become more conscious about what skincare they apply to their skin in pregnancy, as certain ingredients can potentially be absorbed into the bloodstream and reach the developing baby. Natural skincare offers a gentler approach that can address pregnancy-related skin concerns while minimizing exposure to potentially harmful chemicals.

Using Natural Skincare During Pregnancy

The concern about conventional skincare during pregnancy stems from compelling scientific evidence about skin absorption. Our skin is not simply a barrier but a permeable organ—studies show that certain chemicals in personal care products can enter the bloodstream through dermal absorption. During pregnancy, these substances may cross the placental barrier and reach the developing fetus whose detoxification systems are still immature.

Conventional skincare products often contain potentially problematic ingredients. This could include

  • Parabens

  • Phthalates

  • chemical sunscreens.

Some of these compounds have been identified as endocrine disruptors that may interfere with hormonal balance—particularly concerning during pregnancy when hormonal regulation is crucial for fetal development. For example, retinoids, commonly found in anti-aging products, have been linked to birth defects when used in oral form, raising caution about topical application as well.

During pregnancy, hormonal fluctuations can also often lead to skin changes such as increased sensitivity, dryness, or the infamous "pregnancy glow." Some women also experience melasma (darkening patches on the face), acne, or stretch marks. While these changes are normal, they prompt many to reassess their skincare routines. Adopting a clean and organic pregnancy skincare routine is crucial to be able to protect yourself during this time of your life.


How Does Skin Change During Pregnancy?

It’s not new news at all - pregnancy-related skin changes are vast and vary for every woman. Hormones, and fluctuations in your cardiovascular system, can cause skin changes.

Some people may have 9 months of flawless skin. Others can have unfavorable skin conditions during their pregnancy.

Some of the most common skin changes to account for in any pregnancy survival kit include -

  1. Melasma: Hormonal fluctuations can cause pigmentation or darkened patches, known as melasma. This "mask of pregnancy,” develops in 50% of pregnancies during the second trimester.
  2. Acne: If you thought your teen years were bad, then pregnancy might take you by surprise. Increased oiliness due to the rise in progesterone can cause acne in the first trimester. These breakouts do improve in the second trimester.
  3. Spider Veins: The increase in blood volume can cause the appearance of spider veins on the lower legs of pregnant women. Spider veins often reverse postpartum. But, compression socks are a good addition to a pregnancy survival kit to prevent their occurrence.

  4. Stretch Marks: Expanding areas of the skin, such as the breasts and belly, can see stretch marks, regardless of oil application.

Skincare Ingredients for Your Pregnancy Survival Kit

A minimal and organic skincare routine for your skin that does not leave you worried is crucial. Any skincare tips and advice during pregnancy is bound to come in handy.

Here are some ingredients that we view as must-haves in any pregnancy survival kit.

  1. Rosehip Seed Oil for Pregnancy :

We often get this question - Is Rosehip Seed Oil safe for pregnancy? Rosehip seed oil offers numerous benefits for pregnancy-related skin concerns. Rosehip Seed Oil has a lot of carotenoids. This type of vitamin A derivative possesses anti-aging, skin-brightening, and skin-regenerating qualities. Rosehip Seed Oil is a pregnancy-safe multi-tasker that reduces fine lines and pigmentation during pregnancy.

  • Rich in vitamins A, C, and E rosehip helps combat hyperpigmentation issues like melasma due to hormonal changes in pregnancy

  • Significantly improved skin moisture and elasticity while reducing inflammation—particularly valuable for stretched skin during pregnancy

  • Helps maintain the skin barrier function that often becomes compromised during pregnancy

Rosehip Seed Oil for pregnancy is definitely a favorite due to its incredible benefits as well as its gentle effect on the skin.

Empress Naturals offers 100% pure and high-quality Rosehip Seed Oil sourced from Chile, South America.


  1. Prickly Pear Seed Oil for Pregnancy :

With nutrient-rich antioxidants, prickly pear seed oil for pregnancy is moisturizing, providing intense hydration to the skin. It can help reduce the appearance of hyperpigmentation, melasma, and dark spots.

  • A powerful antioxidant with more than 150% levels of Vitamin E as compared to argan oil, prickly pear protects pregnant skin from environmental damage

  • Prickly pear promotes wound healing and collagen synthesis, which is beneficial for preventing and minimizing stretch marks during pregnancy

The oil has soothing properties that can ease skin irritation, redness, and inflammation. It is particularly effective at providing relief to sensitive skin conditions during pregnancy. Despite its nourishing benefits, prickly pear seed oil is lightweight. It gets absorbed by the skin, leaving it soft, smooth, and non-greasy.

Empress Naturals' 100% pure and high-quality Moroccan Gold Prickly Pear Seed Oil is just what your skin needs!


  1. Argan Oil for Pregnancy:

Argan oil, often referred to as "liquid gold," Argan Oil has been used for centuries by Berber women for its remarkable beauty and healing properties during pregnancy. Rich in vitamin E, essential fatty acids, and antioxidants, argan oil has gained worldwide recognition in recent years as a versatile skincare ingredient in pregnancy. It offers multiple benefits for expectant mothers dealing with the unique challenges pregnancy poses to their skin.

  • Increases skin elasticity—a critical benefit during pregnancy when the skin must stretch to accommodate a growing belly. I

  • Rich content of vitamin E and ferulic acid provides antioxidant protection while helping to maintain hydration levels.

  • Argan oil's efficacy in improving skin hydration and skin barrier function, addressing the dryness many women experience during pregnancy

In fact Argan Oil is also known to be incredible for perimenopausal skin, solidifying its versatility.


Ingredients to Avoid During Pregnancy

First things first - your doctor is the best person to tell you what is good for you to use during pregnancy. Yet, there are some ingredients that are avoided due to their harmful effects.

Cosmetic items must be "safe" based on their specific applications and labeling. But, the FDA does not mandate clearance before the sale of these products.

This raises serious concerns about which cosmetics are safe to use during pregnancy. Most experts (and hence we) err along the side of caution.

1. Retinol

Vitamin A supports good skin, immunological, reproductive, and eye health. When you ingest it or absorb it via your skin, your body turns it into retinol.

Some anti-aging skin care products contain retinoids, a form of retinol. It has become a holy grail due to its ability to cure acne and diminish fine wrinkles. But, some studies have found retinoids to cause birth irregularities. Hence, doctors advise against their use during pregnancy.

Read further to know pregnancy-safe ingredients to use instead for similar skin results.

2. Hydroquinone

Hydroquinone lightens pigmentation caused by melasma and chloasma during pregnancy. There remains no confirmed relationship between hydroquinone and serious birth abnormalities.

Yet, the body absorbs a large amount of hydroquinone (35% to 45%) compared to other ingredients. Hence, doctors are against its use during pregnancy.

3. Phthalates

Phthalates are endocrine-disruptive chemicals present in many cosmetics and personal care items. Cosmetics are the most common cause of phthalate exposure.

Phthalate exposure has been related to major developmental problems in human studies. Phthalates, also abundant in plastic packaging, can seep into personal care products.

Pregnancy Skincare Routine At a Glance

Morning (AM Routine)

  1. Gentle Cleanse: Use a mild, fragrance-free cleanser with natural ingredients like aloe or oat extract.

  2. Treat: Use the AM Serum to help with pregnancy-related dark spots and dryness.

  3. Protect: Apply mineral sunscreen with zinc oxide or titanium dioxide (SPF 30+).

Evening (PM Routine)

  1. Cleanse: Remove makeup with coconut oil if needed, then use a gentle cleanser.

  2. Treat: Use the PM Serum to help with pregnancy-related dull skin, and hyperpigmentation, and to detoxify skin.

  3. Belly Care: Massage cocoa butter or the Calming Massage Oil helps nourish the skin and prevent stretch marks.

Weekly

  • Gentle exfoliation 1-2 times weekly with oatmeal or rice powder

  • Hydrating mask with yogurt, honey, or avocado

Avoid

  • Retinoids

  • Hydroquinone

  • High-dose salicylic acid

  • Artificial fragrances

  • Chemical sunscreens

Always patch test new products, as pregnancy can change skin sensitivity.

posts.title: PFAs and PEGs in Cosmetics: The Toxic Ingredients Clean Skincare Avoids
posts.excerpt: Recognizing harmful ingredients in skincare can sometimes feel impossible, especially the ones that are most harmful. Some of your favorite products likely contain these scary...
posts.content:
Recognizing harmful ingredients in skincare can sometimes feel impossible, especially the ones that are most harmful. Some of your favorite products likely contain these scary things, whether it be waterproof mascara or an incredibly lathery shampoo.

If you are someone who is seeking out a way to improve their skincare regimen and eliminate products with toxic chemicals, PFAs and PEGs are two common ingredients in cosmetics that can be bad in skincare and should be avoided as their potential negatives disproportionately outweigh the positives.

What are PFAs?

PFAs, short for perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, can be found in many waterproof, sweatproof, and long-lasting cosmetics. They are characterized by a strong bond between fluorine and carbon. Interestingly, these chemicals aren’t only found in cosmetics– they are also used in waterproof clothing, nonstick kitchenware, and plastic food containers.

They are commonly referred to as “forever chemicals” because of how slowly they decompose, and therefore accumulate in the human body and the environment for an extended period of time. This compound is used to make products more water resistant, durable, and spreadable.

What are PEGs?

PEGs, or polyethylene glycols, are compounds that are in shampoos, deodorants, moisturizers, facial cleansers, and many other cosmetics. They are used in skincare as thickening agents, solvents, softeners, and moisture carriers. Many cosmetic brands have gravitated towards this specific chemical compound because of its ability to target moisture and hydrate the skin. They're also used in pharmaceuticals as laxatives, and contain chemicals that were used as mustard gas in WWI!

Why are PFAs & PEGs Harmful?

These chemical compounds are known to be incredibly toxic and have been linked to serious health problems.

The CDC has linked PFAs to serious kidney, liver, developmental, and reproductive issues. More recently, it has also been discussed how PFAs can affect the antibody response to vaccinations, such as those for COVID-19. Because of the controversy surrounding PFAs, a few states are discussing taking action to ban the use of these chemicals in cosmetics.

PEGs are notorious for containing carcinogens and respiratory irritants. Their potency can cause skin irritation, and can cause serious problems if applied on broken skin or open wounds. In addition to that, long-term exposure has been linked to damage to the nervous system.

What are PEGs in Skincare

Being able to correctly identify these chemicals in the products that you use is important, so that you are aware of what you are putting on your skin, and into your body as well. Here is a little cheat sheet you can use while reading ingredient lists –

  • High levels of fluorine are a strong indicator of the use of PFAs
  • Products that claim to be waterproof or long-wearing likely contain PFAs
  • PTFE (polytetrafluoroethylene)
  • Perfluorooctyl triethoxysilane
  • Perfluorononyl dimethicone
  • Perfluorodecalin
  • Perfluorohexane
  • Some PFAs are listed as SVHC substances
  • PEGs could be listed as glycols
  • PEGs are often listed with a number next to them to represent the approximate molecular weight
    • The lower the number, the easier it is for it to penetrate the skin
  • Stearic acid or PEG 100 stearate are common names for PEGs

What to do next?

In conclusion, PFAs and PEGs simply do not offer enough benefits for their harmful attributes to be overlooked. Learning how to identify them in skincare may seem daunting, but it can protect you from scary things such as kidney, liver, and reproductive issues, to name a few.

Simply put, no product is worth risking your health over, so why not consider going the natural/healthier route? You can easily attain all of your desired skin care goals without using products that have PFAs and PEGs.

posts.title: Toxic Ingredients Hiding in Your Skincare & Why Clean Skincare Matters
posts.excerpt: In the United States, cosmetics are loosely regulated by the FDA– it is not required by law for most ingredients to be approved before they...
posts.content:
In the United States, cosmetics are loosely regulated by the FDA– it is not required by law for most ingredients to be approved before they are released on the market, which is the exact reason as to why there are so many scary ingredients floating around out in our everyday products.

Essentially, there are two laws that pertain to cosmetics in the United States: the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, and the Fair Packaging and Labeling Act. Both acts basically require that products be labeled and include ample directions for safety purposes.

Unfortunately for consumers and cosmetic shoppers, this leaves a lot of space for toxic and harmful products and ingredients to be used as long as the ingredients are disclosed, and the product shows how to safely use it.

A FEW TOXIC CHEMICALS AND CARCINOGENS THAT ARE LEGAL IN THE USA

  • Formaldehyde and formaldehyde releasing preservatives (FRPs) can be found in products like nail polish, eye shadow, mascara, and various shampoos. It is used to prevent bacteria growth and to prolong the shelf life of products.
    Although the amount of FPRs is limited to a concentration of up to 0.2%, it is still worth mentioning that exposure to larger amounts can lead to irritation of the eyes, nose, throat, and the respiratory system as a whole.
    The use of it is strictly prohibited in Japan, and is also restricted in the EU by the European Commission.

  • Parabens are definitely one of the most talked about ingredients to avoid, and already have an incredibly negative reputation in the world of cosmetics, unsurprisingly.
    They appear in moisturizers, shampoos, spray tan products, and shaving creams to name just a few. Various parabens can cause breast and skin cancer, and can even decrease sperm count in men.
    This one is particularly unsettling because of how powerfully they can upset your hormones, disrupt growth, development, and reproduction.

  • Triclosan can be found in antibacterial soaps, toothpastes, deodorants, shower gels, and other products that claim to have antimicrobial properties. This chemical that is also used as a preservative and a preventative measure against bacterial growth, has been linked to thyroid issues upon long term exposure.
    Newer studies have also shown that it is possible that     triclosan can actually create bacteria resistant to antibiotics. A number of studies are looking into the development of skin cancer after prolonged exposure to triclosan, which is really scary to think about.

  • Phenacetin was previously used as a pain reliever and fever reducer until it was banned by the FDA in 1983 because of its carcinogenic properties. The fact that it is no longer used as a drug but is instead used in smaller amounts in cosmetics is deeply concerning!
    Products containing bleach like facial hair dyes and hair removal agents often utilize phenacetin. It can cause health problems like kidney failure and anemia, and can even be linked to malignant mammary tumors.

  • Coal tar is a known carcinogen, and a product of coal processing. It is used in a wide range of products from hair dyes, shampoos, dandruff treatments, and skin redness treatments. It is associated with cancers of the lungs, bladder, kidney, and digestive tract.
    If you need yet another reason to be turned off from this chemical, it is also considered to be toxic to aquatic animals– not only is it bad for us, it can seriously and negatively impact the environment as well.

  • Benzene is a toxin that can cause harm by being inhaled and ingested, and is used in the production of plastics, hair conditioners, and hair styling products.
    Occupational exposure to benzene has been linked to leukemia, and can cause serious damage to the eyes, skin, respiratory system, nervous system, and blood.
    The European Commission prohibits its use in cosmetics, which makes you wonder why it is still used in the United States.

Other ingredients to look out for include silicones, which are often said to be more than they actually are, or PEGs that are causing harm to your skin.

Massage.

The word evokes memories of luxurious spa treatments that are curated to relax your body and mind. But in a time where skincare has taken the reins on our mainstream interpretation of self-care, most of us are starting to neglect the benefits derived from a serene massage.

What if we said that you can gain the benefits of exotic massages from the comfort of your home using the right techniques, tricks, and oils. In fact, the history of massages dates back to ancient times wherein individuals used naturally occurring oils to massage themselves to heal ailments and experience the tranquility that came alongside.

Oil massage may appear to be a messy and laborious technique better left to the professionals, but once you've mastered the fundamentals, self-massage with the right ingredients and oils will become an unmissable part of your self-care routine and well worth the effort.

In this article, we will be taking a walk through history to understand the origins of massage oil culture across the globe and its benefits as backed up by modern science. Read on to learn more about self-massage and the different kinds of oils you can incorporate into your self-care routine.

Massage Oils Have Been a Staple in Self-Care for Centuries

We may think that the peak of popularity for massage therapy is approaching in this post-COVID 19 era. The truth is that the healing benefits of massage have been a part of holistic healing practices for ages. This traces back to multiple cultures across the globe.

Eastern Origin of Massage Healing

Massage treatments date back to beyond 3000 BCE in India. It was regarded as a holy natural healing practice. Hindus passed down Massage treatments and techniques for generations. It was an Ayurvedic "life health" medicine to heal injuries, reduce pain, and prevent/cure diseases.

In Ayurveda, practitioners believed that illness and disease occur when individuals are out of harmony with their surroundings. A massage would help restore by bringing cohesion to the body's natural and physical equilibrium.

Some time around 2700 BCE, Massage therapeutic practices spread to Southeast Asia. Chinese massage methods evolved from the abilities and practices of ancient Chinese medicine, martial arts, and Buddhist and Taoist spiritual yoga training. Their methods were quite similar to the Indians'. It was based on the concept that imbalance or lack of energy causes sickness.

Massage practice in Middle Eastern culture

We know from tomb paintings that massage treatment had reached its way to Egypt by 2500 BCE. The Egyptians developed reflexology, which includes applying pressure to certain zones on the hands and feet to influence healing.

Western culture's tryst with massage practice

By around 700-800 BCE, Western Civilization adopted massages as a therapeutic practice. Infact, the ‘father of medicine’, Hippocrates was apparently the first to prescribe his patients a combination of massages. Along with it, he prescribed a proper diet, exercise, music, and fresh air. As per him, it was a way of helping them restore their inner balance. Sounds eerily similar to what we view to be our self-care routines today, doesn't it?

Benefits of Your Daily Self-Massage

When looked at today as a staple in self-care practices, a massage can range from the most luxurious and elaborate process to a simple and concise daily practice to heal aching joints and calm the aura around your physical body. The benefits can encompass a whole array of things but some basic benefits of self-massage are described below:

You're revitalizing both your mind and body

Whether you choose to start or end your day with a simple 10-minute self-massage - it doesn't matter. It becomes an opportunity for you to collect your thoughts and reframe your mind while you nourish your body.

If you've been feeling intellectually or physically sluggish, a more vigorous massage stroke will revitalize your body and mind. On the other hand, a more gradual self-massage focused on your breath assists your mind in engaging with the moment and your body in relaxing.

You are finally calm while sleeping

The experience of self-massage is highly stimulating to your brain, neurological system, mind, soul and skin. It calms nerves and promotes deeper and better sleep. The massage creates the sensation of being surrounded in loving arms.

It mimics warmth and has a significant influence on how you feel towards yourself and everything around you.

It has the ability to replace your worries with a sensation of happiness and tranquility leaving you feeling at peace. A consistent routine involving self-massage will assist you in falling asleep and remaining asleep throughout the night.

You are cleansing your body's main organ

More often than not, we fall ill because we are constantly in contact with toxins in our immediate physical environment be it at work, home, school or the gym. We are ill as a result of the buildup of poisons.

Massage your body to encourage the removal of pollutants and toxins via your skin. Proper elimination is essential for feeling invigorated and ready to actively engage in life.

You’re achieving youthful skin

We often forget that while our facial skin appears to age the fastest, our body skin is also aging and requires the right care too! Body oils and massage treatments protect your skin from harsh environmental influences, keeping it smooth and supple.

The right oil will hydrate and lubricate the skin and you will notice an improvement in resistance to dehydration, flaking, and even bruising. Self-massage boosts skin's inherent bright attractiveness, allowing it to age gracefully and eliminate wrinkles.

You’ll improve your internal blood circulation

Self-Massage promotes blood circulation, allowing metabolic wastes to be removed quickly and offering a boost in energy and endurance much needed to cope with your busy life. It also holds the ability to nourish internal organs and improves their functionality.

It also rejuvenates and energizes the body's cells. Take care of the single most crucial thing you have control over: yourself.

Self-Massage has physical, emotional, and psychic repercussions, which is intriguing. Everyone deserves to be cared for and loved. It gives you the opportunity to engage in some much needed downtime, leaving you with a sense of warmth, comfort, and confidence - the perfect end to any self-care ritual.

Some Tips & Tricks for your next Self-Massage

Tip No. 1: Taking the very first steps to conduct a self-massage with body oil might be intimidating, so pick a more relaxed day if you're doing it for the first time.

Tip No. 2: You can warm the oil a little before starting your massage by placing it in a water bath. This will help relax your muscles that extra bit and release any tension you may be carrying in them. Use the Body Kansa Wand to help you go one step further in detoxification

Tip No. 3: After your self-massage, make sure to wear socks or cover your feet in some way so you avoid ruining your floors with any residual oil.

Learn how to self-massage with our Comprehensive Guide on Abhyanga

Our CEO, Elizabeth’s Hot Tip: Massage oil into your skin, starting at the feet and working your way towards your heart. Long strokes on the arms and legs and round strokes on any joints are recommended.

Broad, clockwise circular strokes should be used to massage the belly and chest. If you don't have a massage oil (go get one at empressnaturals.co!), you can also use Rosehip Seed Oil as it absorbs straight into your skin, leaving no sticky feeling behind.

Ultimate Guide to an Organic Summer Skincare Routine

We've never met anyone who has qualms with summer. It's a season that effortlessly captivates with its irresistible charm. The air becomes warm and inviting, carrying a refreshing breeze that delicately whispers through the trees. Bringing along a sense of tranquility and serenity.

Beaches come alive with the symphony of laughter, the rhythmic crashing of waves, and the nostalgic scent of sunscreen. Summer is the personification of calm and carefree living. It grants us a much-needed reason to slow down the pace of our chaotic lives. It's a time to unwind, reconnect with nature, and savor the simple pleasures that make life truly beautiful.

Summer, with its carefree and blissful vibe, also reminds us of the need to commit to a consistent summer skincare routine. This season's warm air and increased sun exposure necessitate extra care. You need a well-thought-out routine to preserve and nurture your skin.

As we spend long days at the beach and participate in outdoor activities, it is critical to prioritize skincare practices that protect us from damaging UV radiation while also keeping our skin healthy and bright. Sun protection, protective clothing, and seeking cover during peak sun hours are all vital in preventing UV damage.

Using summer-specific skin hydration, cleansing, and repairing rituals is CRUCIAL. It keeps your skin regenerated and radiant despite the heat and humidity. By practicing a consistent summer skincare routine, you will not only repair the health of your skin but also witness that sun-kissed glow you crave!


SUMMER SKINCARE ROUTINE NEEDS EXTRA CARE

…especially for mature skin

As the sun shines strongly and temperatures increase, it's essential to prepare your skin. The blazing heat and high humidity can make you more vulnerable and susceptible to certain skin problems that can become exaggerated in the summer. Understanding these challenges and taking proactive measures to address them is essential for an effective anti-aging summer skincare routine!

UV Exposure & Damage

Mature skin is typically thinner and more delicate, making it prone to increased sensitivity and quicker sunburn. Furthermore, accumulated sun exposure over the years can lead to age spots, fine lines, and wrinkles.

The depletion of collagen and elastin, which are responsible for maintaining skin's firmness and elasticity, further compromises its ability to repair itself efficiently post-sun damage. This makes mature skin more prone to sunburn, sunspots, and an increased risk of developing skin cancer.

Empress Tip: Prioritize your sun protection by wearing broad-spectrum sunscreen. Additionally, wear protective clothing, wide-brimmed hats, and UV-blocking sunglasses for comprehensive defense against harmful UV rays.

Excess Oil & Sebum during summer

Excess oil and breakouts can pose particular challenges for women with mature skin. Hormonal changes associated with aging can disrupt the delicate balance of sebum production, leading to increased oiliness and a higher likelihood of clogged pores.

Additionally, the decrease in skin cell turnover and natural exfoliation can contribute to a buildup of dead skin cells, further exacerbating the potential for breakouts. As a result, mature skin may experience a combination of oiliness, congestion, and the occasional hormonal acne flare-ups.

It becomes crucial to address these issues with a gentle yet effective skincare routine that targets excess oil, promotes a clear complexion, and supports the skin's natural balance.

Dehydrated & Dull Skin during summer

The skin's natural capacity to retain moisture reduces as we age, resulting in increased dryness and a damaged moisture barrier. Mature skin is prone to losing moisture, resulting in a rough, dull, and flaky appearance.

The decline in natural oil production further contributes to this problem, making it more difficult for the skin to maintain optimal moisture levels. Factors such as sun exposure, harsh weather conditions, and certain medications can further exacerbate dryness in mature skin.

posts.content:
What does the word self-care mean to you?

Does it evoke the instant recollection of smells & sights synonymous with a calm environment? Mood lighting, relaxing music, and possibly your favorite unwinding activity - skincare.

It’s no secret that skincare has taken over the world by storm. And honestly, it’s no surprise. Our face is our primary identity and often the first impression upon a stranger. Who wouldn't want to take care of and pamper themselves?

And who would say no to some “me-time pampering” (not us, for sure)! But it raises the question of whether skincare is indeed self-care.

Is it possible to actually comfort and soothe yourself by practicing mindfulness throughout your skincare regimen, or are we simply mistaken, confusing self-indulgence for self-care? Is your self-care all about the face, or are you actually taking care of yourself and giving yourself the time to reflect?

In this article, we cover the aspects of self-care you might be neglecting and leaving you thinking a little deeper about how you make time for yourself.

CAN SKINCARE BE SELF-CARE?

Well, that depends on how you define self-care. At its core, self-care means protecting your own health, happiness, and wellbeing by doing whatever it takes. Logically, that implies that a self-care routine would have to be unique for each individual to truly protect them. There is no “right or wrong” in self-care.

Ultimately, your self-care routine has only one objective: to make you feel like the most rejuvenated version of yourself! Making the effort to care for yourself can come in so many different forms, but they have similar goals and benefits:

A Continued Sense of Control

In today's fast-paced and chaotic world, your skincare self-care regimen serves as a constant, giving you a sense of control in the face of uncontrolled events. Maintaining a steady schedule may therefore help minimize your chance of developing numerous mood disorders.

Inculcating Mindfulness

Your skincare regimen provides you with a window of opportunity to calm your mind - to appreciate the emotions you are experiencing in the current moment, or other emotions that were too busy to notice throughout your day, without harsh judgment. This helps you organize your thoughts and find a moment of peace for yourself.

Improved Self-Esteem

Mindfulness isn't just good for your emotional well-being. It has also been proven in studies to reduce collagen-damaging stress hormones, strengthen the skin barrier, "switch off" inflammatory genes (which lead to premature skin aging indications), and promote lymphatic drainage.

And keep in mind, these correlating benefits of skincare have nothing to do with the actual benefits your skincare products will leave on your skin! Glowing and healthy skin will only build upon your confidence.

Elizabeth’s Pro Tip: Don't Limit Skincare to Your Face!

Your skin plays a huge part in your daily functioning because and fun fact, your skin is the largest organ of your body! But when we talk about skincare and self-care, we often only refer to the skin on our face.

Well, there's a lot more to taking care of your skin than just your face! Just as your face is exposed to the sun and pollution every day, so are your arms and legs! Prioritize them as part of your self-care routine!

4 ESSENTIALS FOR YOUR NEXT SELF-CARE RITUAL!

We’ve talked about how important it is to remain engaged in what you do as part of your self-care routine. Well, here are some ways that can help you remember to do just that the next time you decide to do a little ‘me-time pampering’.


Don’t rush it

The best way to stay engaged is to give yourself the indulgence of taking at minimum a few moments of "me-time" to care for your skin. Remind yourself that your complete skincare regimen, from cleanser to sunscreen, takes no more than 15 minutes, so there's no need to rush through it.

Pay attention to all of your sensations

For example, while you massage your cleanser to remove the grime of the day, take note of every sensation. Pay attention to how your body oil nourishes and seeps into your skin, leaving it glowing and moisturized from within. It is normal for your mind to wander at this stage. When you notice your thoughts straying, acknowledge it and return to the current moment.


Avoid using electronic devices

In the latter stages of our skincare process, most of us tend to distract ourselves by streaming content on Netflix or listening to a podcast (e.g., when using serums or moisturizers). However, they inhibit you from really engaging in the current moment. So, regard your skincare routine as precious self-care time, and put aside your phone, iPad, or laptop. Spend some time alone with yourself.

Relax by listening to soothing music

Listening to calming music may assist in increasing your capacity to practice mindfulness, hence strengthening the latter's mood-boosting and stress-relieving effects. Remember that the main word here is relaxation. That implies calm beats are better, and lyrics should be avoided if feasible.

ALL SELF-CARE IS NOT SKINCARE!

As much as skincare forms an essential part of our self-care routine, it is crucial to realize that it is not all that self-care consists of!

While skincare is a valid method of indulging in self-care, there is a certain thing about applying many products that can actually affect the health of your skin. Meaning? You shouldn't go overboard with adding things to your routine in order to extend your mindfulness practice.

Too many products can cause reactions your skin may not be prepared for! Why?

One of the primary reasons is that the more products you apply, the more likely it is that you're combining skincare actives that don't work well together.

FINAL VERDICT

So, yes, self-care can be skincare. However, it is important to note that this is not the only type of self-care you may practice.

Instead, self-care is a comprehensive way of life that can involve both minor (such as making time for your skincare regimen) and major (such as adopting better lifestyle practices) activities. There are several self-care methods available to help you keep physically, psychologically, and emotionally well.

Whatever self-care activities you choose, staying consistent with them is critical. Begin your self-care journey slowly by selecting one technique to add to your everyday life.

Let's assume you want to include mindfulness in your self-care routine. Build up to doing this every day for a week, then add more self-care routines when you're ready.

OUR SELF-CARE FAVORITES!

When we think about self-care, we think of luxury. And that is exactly what Empress Naturals is all about. Here are some of our favorite products that are self-care must-haves!

posts.content:
Essential oils for menopause and perimenopause symptoms

Intense hot flashes. Night sweats. Insomnia. Increased anxiety. When you begin to experience these symptoms, you know you are approaching menopause.

We all know it's a rite of passage; a life change experience signaling a new stage that almost every woman will go through at some point in life. Sure, it's natural, and we all go through it... but it doesn't have to be so taxing on your body, mind, or psyche!

There are so many ways for you to tackle these menopause symptoms! But if you’re not one of those who likes taking medication for everything and is obsessed with natural and holistic methods of healing, you’ve landed in the absolute right place.

Massage therapy

Let us introduce you to the ancient practice of massage therapy that is renowned for its incredible benefits for the skin and overall health.

It is well-known for its ability to relieve pain, promote healing, and improve relaxation; all absolute necessities when dealing with challenging menopause symptoms.

Massage therapy with essential oils has been known to ease some of the bothersome symptoms of menopause and perimenopause by boosting blood flow, decreasing muscle tension, and promoting relaxation in your space.

So, the next time you have a hot flash or are experiencing mood swings, give a massage a serious thought! It will not only benefit your physical health, but it will also do wonders for your mental health!

But, let's be honest, not everyone has the time to get a professional massage every time we have a hot flash. There's also a different kind of magic to establishing a relaxing night time routine where you spend some time taking care of yourself inside-out!

This is where self-massage comes into play. Yes, you can totally massage yourself! All it needs is a little know-how and some fundamental skills. You have to check out our self-care massage routine where we lay out all the techniques to massage each body part correctly!

So, whether you schedule a professional massage or pamper yourself, don't underestimate the therapeutic benefits of massage therapy during menopause!

What are the symptoms of menopause?

The main menopausal symptom is, of course, irregular periods, which eventually lead to the cessation of menstruation.

Menopause is said to have officially started when menstruation ceases for a year. In addition, when estrogen levels fall, so does fertility. Perimenopause can cause vaginal dryness and other difficulties in women.

Hot flashes and nighttime sweats are two of the most widely recognised and prevalent menopausal symptoms.

These nocturnal sweats can be disruptive to sleep, but anxiety and sleeplessness during menopause can also make getting a decent night's sleep difficult. This lack of sleep can lead to moodiness and depression, as well as impaired memory and attention.

Menopause can induce sleep difficulties in addition to emotional changes. Women may struggle to get asleep, stay asleep, or wake up prematurely in the morning.

These sleep disruptions can be caused by hot flashes, which can create night sweats and make it difficult to sleep, or by worry or sadness, which can interfere with sleep quality.

Sleep disruptions may have a serious influence on a woman's physical and mental well-being. Sleep deprivation can cause weariness, irritation, and difficulties concentrating throughout the day.

Menopause symptoms such as mood swings, irritation, and depression can have a substantial influence on a woman's mental well-being. Menopausal hormonal shifts can impact the concentrations of serotonin and dopamine, which can lead to mood swings.

Mood swings

They are characterized by abrupt and severe emotional changes, such as moving from pleased to sad or furious in a short amount of time. These mood swings can be challenging to control, particularly when they occur frequently and disrupt everyday routines.

Depression

It is another typical menopausal symptom, caused by the hormonal balances accompanied during this time. You may feel sad, helpless, and even a loss of interest in doing activities you previously loved to do. In cases of severe hindrance to your daily activities, make sure you get the professional guidance you need.

Massage Therapy Benefits for Menopause


Essential oils for Improved Sleep and Relaxation

According to research, regular massage treatments (we always recommend self-massage to remain consistent) result in improved sleep, decreased pain, and less anxiety among menopausal women.

Furthermore, using essential oils during massage could further enhance these advantages .

Lavender oil has been shown to aid with relaxation and sleep quality, whilst bergamot essential oil can help with tension and anxiety.

Ylang ylang oil has a relaxing impact on the body and can aid in the promotion of a good night's sleep.

Women can increase their sleep quantity and quality by adding frequent massage treatment with essential oils for their menopause symptoms. Better sleep also means better emotional and mental wellbeing; 8 hours of restful sleep can cure a lot of unnecessary symptoms of menopause!

Empress Product Picks: Our Luxury Calming Massage Oil is the perfect formulation for your menopausal rest and relaxation needs! With Lavender, Ylang Ylang, and Mandarin Essential Oil, we call it relaxation in a bottle.

Essential oils for reduced Hot Flashes

Self-massage can help minimize hot flashes by increasing relaxation and stress reduction. Hot flashes are known to be triggered by stress, and massaging has been demonstrated to relieve tension and increase relaxation.

The right combination of essential oils can be a gamechanger to reduce those pesky hot flashes during menopause.

Peppermint oil, for instance, has cooling properties that can help alleviate hot flashes, while clary sage oil has estrogen-like properties that can help regulate hormonal imbalances.

Try to DIY it

posts.title: An ultimate guide to self massage at home
posts.excerpt: Unlock Your Body's Potential: Master Self-Massage with These Simple Techniques Nothing beats the feeling of total serenity after you’ve wrapped up a long week and...
posts.content:
Unlock Your Body's Potential: Master Self-Massage with These Simple Techniques

Nothing beats the feeling of total serenity after you’ve wrapped up a long week and are eagerly waiting on some TLC on the weekend.

After all, self-care has been (and rightfully so!) one of the biggest growing trends during and after the COVID-19 pandemic. We think that if there’s anything we should always remind ourselves, it’s that we need to listen to our bodies when they need us to slow down.

Our world changed in so many ways during the pandemic and in our quest to adapt efficiently, we forgot to stop and keep track of all the habits we had that benefited our overall health.

Hours spent in front of your laptop at an uncomfortable angle, strained eyes from the blue light, the lack of circulation in your feet due to the lack of movement all day - are all changes that have been occurring due to the predominantly work-from-home lifestyle we’ve gotten sucked into.

If the last three years have flown past you and you haven’t had the opportunity to finally take care of yourself, let this be your reminder : Your body and soul both need some time off.


When we realize we’re due for a break, we follow a simple reset routine complete with the best self-massage. Why massage ? Well because it not only alleviates pain from aching shoulders but also resets for the upcoming week.


“You can hold tension and stress in body parts without even realizing it. A self-massage is the perfect way for you to not only recognise which body parts are heavier, stiffer than necessary but also help you relieve that pain in a calming and soothing way. That’s what a self-massage means to me”,

says Elizabeth Koshy, CEO Empress Naturals.

A Self-Massage Can Significantly Improve Your Weekend. Here’s how

It’s no secret that your body and mind are always in sync. But that's not limited to only the good things!

Any imbalances will also have a relapsed effect on the other. An established self-care massage routine has the ability to build your self-awareness in ways that impact more than just your physical symptoms of stress.

Self-massage helps to rejuvenate the blood circulation system, which means your body is getting the necessary levels of oxygen and glucose to various cells while also removing toxins. A self-massage can have the following benefits for your overall well being, making it the perfect addition to your self-care routine.

A self-massage like most other self-care practices is an act of love, for yourself and your body. It helps you get in touch with your body and understand it from a perspective of giving instead of taking.

Whatever you're going through, self-massage will bring you back into balance. It boosts circulation, enhances organ function, aids in detoxification, and improves sleep. It also offers several additional possible advantages, such as delaying aging, moisturizing your skin, and decreasing injury.

It's among the most effective methods to relieve modern-day worry while still leaving your skin smooth and luminous.


“I rarely go to the spa. It’s always a great experience whenever I do but a self-massage is always something I pick over the spa. It’s an intimate experience that makes me feel like I have spent some time understanding everything my mind and body went through over the week. I spend time massaging my body and in return get feedback on how the week has impacted me physically and emotionally. It's a great time for reflection while I prepare myself for the week ahead”,
says Elizabeth.

ELIZABETHS’ STEP-BY-STEP SELF MASSAGE ROUTINE

#1 PREP YOUR SPACE

When you walk into the gym, you’re not starting your workout without setting up your playlist, are you? It's the same way with your self massage routine. Your space is just as important as the actual act of massage. It plays a role in calming and unwinding.

Light a candle, play some relaxing music, switch on the TV to watch a feel-good movie- the world is your oyster.

Once you've set the mood, it's time to make sure all the things you need for your self-massage are close by. Nothing ruins flow more than having to walk up the stairs to get your gua sha in the middle of your massage.

Elizabeth keeps her massage oils (one for calming and one for circulation), guasha, hot stones for her feet, and sleep time tea (any tea with chamomile!) with her before every self-massage.

#2 START FROM HEAD TO TOE

Once you have all the things you need for your massage, Elizabeth recommends starting from head to toe.

Empress Tip
You don't have to do a full-body self-massage every time! You can break it up into parts and do necessary ones when needed!

Head Self-Massage

  1. Begin by using your hands or a massage tool to apply medium pressure on the top of your head.
  2. If you're applying oils, first dip the tips of your fingers in the oil before beginning the massage.
  3. In little circular strokes, massage your scalp. Massage for 5 minutes, covering your entire head.

Neck Self-Massage

  1. Bring your right hand to the bottom of your neck, where it joins your shoulder.
  2. Press your index and middle finger into your neck. Move your fingers up towards the base of your scalp while maintaining pressure.

  3. Continue for another 20 to 30 seconds. Repeat on the other side of your neck.

Shoulder Self-Massage

  1. Middle trapezius: Locate your middle trapezius with your fingertips; it's the area on your back where your neck and shoulders meet. Apply pressure for 10 to 30 seconds before releasing. Repeat this action as required until you feel pain relief or a deeper level of relaxation.

  2. Upper trapezius: Bend your right arm at the elbow so that you can feel your upper trapezius on the right side. Begin with your fingers on the outside of your trap muscle, aka your shoulders, and work your way inside, towards your neck. Repeat gently several times until you feel your trap muscles loosen. Do the same thing on your right side.

Hamstring Self-Massage

For a hamstring self-massage, you can choose to use either your fingers or a foam roller.

  1. With fingers: Use your thumb as the primary pressure on your hamstrings while the other fingers help you keep your leg stable. Drag your thumb upwards towards your knee.
  2. With foam roller: Face up on the foam roller, with the roller beneath your butt. Place your hands behind you on the floor. Roll slowly towards your knee, then return to the starting position directly below your butt.

#3 KEEP IN MIND

A few words of caution before you dive in and begin massaging your neck with wild enthusiasm.

Massage each region for 30 seconds to one minute to lessen the likelihood of becoming sore later on. Be attentive of the body parts you intend to massage so that you do not inadvertently injure yourself.

3 GOLDEN RULES OF SELF-MASSAGE

  • Choose the best oil combinations for your massage.
    Every oil, whether carrier or essential, has a function in your massage. Find products that contain the correct oils for your requirements, or DIY your own. Use our     guide to help you choose the best massage oil for you!
  • Heat the oil.
    This is standard procedure for most self-massages, regardless of the oil used. It helps you to compensate for any characteristics in the surroundings or on your body, resulting in improved absorption of the massage oil into your skin.

  • Move down from the head.
    You may massage various regions of your body on different days, or you could have a complete body massage every day. (trust us, it only takes 15 minutes). Whatever massage you choose, always begin at the top and work your way down. To encourage direct blood flow, move from your extremities to your heart. Move your feet to your thighs or your neck to your heart.

This luxurious massage oil is the only blend you need to improve circulation from the inside out!

It contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatories such as Juniper Berry, Rosemary, and Sage, which will alleviate toxins from your bloodstream and leave your muscles feeling rejuvenated yet relaxed!

Our Calming Massage Oil is an anti-inflammatory, anti-stress, and anxiety-fighting experience that eliminates all stressors from your body and psyche.

For the ideal leisure experience, our formula infuses detoxifying minerals deep into the epidermis. With the effective blend of Lavender, Ylang Ylang, and Sweet Marjoram, it provides rest and relaxation like never before!

posts.content:
The Prickly Pear Cacti yields an ultra-moisturizing, calming oil known as Prickly Pear Seed Oil, which is rapidly gaining popularity in the skincare industry. This benefits of prickly pear seed oil for the skin has revered for generations to treat anything from burns to infections because of its healing and protective properties.

Prickly Pear Seed Oil is among the most potent, opulent skincare ingredients since it is extremely rich in vitamin E and packed with antioxidants. With its intense benefits and multi-purpose uses, it is no wonder that it has quickly gained popularity as one of the most used and loved facial oils on the market! If you are looking for more reasons to incorporate Prickly Pear Seed Oil into your routine, you have come to the right place!

How is Prickly Pear Seed Oil sourced?

The finest Prickly Pear Seed Oil used in skincare is sourced from Morocco (Empress Natural’s Pure Prickly Pear Seed Oil comes from sustainable women-led farms in Morocco). However, the oil is also native to regions of the United States, Australia, Africa, and South America.

The seeds of the plant are normally manually removed and then processed through sophisticated equipment created especially for this purpose in order to extract the precious oil.

The rich composition and cold-pressed extraction of this oil help improve its efficacy for skin problems.

Fun Fact - Prickly Pear Seed Oil has one of the highest concentrations of Vitamin E!

What are the benefits and uses of Prickly Pear Seed Oil?

While there hasn't yet been enough research conducted around the benefits of Prickly Pear Oil, primary results show the innate benefits of the precious oil for your skin!

  1. Abundant Source of Vitamin E: Free radicals that damage the skin are scavenged by Vitamin E, a natural antioxidant. Vitamin E is abundant in prickly pear oil, which also possesses powerful anti-inflammatory qualities and is thought to quickly heal skin lesions.

  2. Anti-Aging Agent: Prickly pear seed oil, sometimes known as "Moroccan Gold," protects your skin from the numerous aging causes that sneak into it. This magical oil is a great moisturizer and restores the flexibility of your skin by boosting collagen synthesis. The oil gradually gets rid of the obstinate fine lines and wrinkles while also helping to balance out the skin tone.

  3. Providing Hydration: Prickly Pear Seed Oil contains a high composition of linoleic acid. Linoleic Acid is known to keep the skin moisturized and hydrated to prevent water loss and promote water retention.

  4. Brighter Skin: Packed with vital fatty acids, vitamins E and K, present in the oil, increase the creation of collagen, preserving the skin's suppleness and shine. The oil's skin-brightening characteristics function in a special way to give the skin a natural glow. With regular usage of prickly pear oil, hyperpigmentation and dark patches on aging skin can be substantially diminished.


If you want to know more about the incredible potential of this magical oil, make sure you check out our blog on the Amazing Benefits of Prickly Pear Seed Oil.

How does Prickly Pear Seed Oil Help Treat Skin Conditions?


Prickly Pear Oil for Acne

The high composition of Linoleic Acid in prickly pear oil makes it ideal for oily and congested skin. The regenerating qualities of this fatty acid aid in the treatment and prevention of new breakouts.

The presence of Vitamin E in the oil also helps in hastening cell turnover, leading to faster healing of acne. It also reduces hyperpigmentation and evens the texture of the skin.

Prickly Pear Oil for Wrinkles and Fine Lines

Essential fatty acids, antioxidants, and vitamins E and K are abundant in prickly pear seed oil. It is a very helpful oil for mature skin because it hydrates and smooths out wrinkles. It reduces the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles and promotes the development of collagen, leaving you with supple, firm and glowing skin.

Prickly Pear Oil for Eczema

One of the best oils for sensitive skin, Prickly Pear Seed Oil is ideal for people with eczema. High in Vitamin E, the oil moisturizes and nourishes dry or flaky skin and encourages cellular renewal to reduce scarring. With beta-carotenes that act as forerunners to Vitamin A, Prickly Pear Oil helps repair and heal skin.

Prickly Pear Oil for Dark Circles and Under the Eyes

Vitamin E, which repairs skin, and vitamin K, which strengthens capillaries and lessens dark under-eye circles and spider veins, are both present in higher concentrations in Prickly Pear Seed Oil than in Argan Oil. The facial oil minimizes the appearance of dark circles and spider veins while hydrating and balancing the delicate under-eye skin.

Like Cucumber and Argan Seed Oil, Prickly Pear Seed Oil is rich in tocopherol, which is also known as vitamin E. Remarkably, Prickly Pear Seed Oil contains 150% more vitamin E than Argan Oil! Additionally, its high concentration of amino acids makes it a great choice for encouraging collagen production, boosting cell turnover, and diminishing the visibility of fine lines and wrinkles

How Can You Use Prickly Pear Seed Oil?


Prickly Pear Seed Oil can be used on its own as a moisturizer or serum as part of your skincare routine. Take 2-3 drops of pure Prickly Pear Seed Oil in your palm and gently massage the oil into your face using circular motions.

As a carrier oil, Prickly Pear Seed Oil can be mixed with a couple of drops of other essential oils and used on your face, neck, and hair depending on the essential oil added.

What are the Physical Characteristics of Prickly Pear Seed Oil?

Color: 100% Pure, cold-pressed Prickly Pear Seed Oil will always be pale yellow to golden yellow in color. When other oils or ingredients are added to pure Prickly Pear Seed Oil, the color is bound to change but an understanding of the original color helps customers buy the right products.

Smell: The smell of prickly pear oil is comparable to that of the ripe, citrusy fresh fruit. At times, it can also smell nutty alike to the smell of Argan Oil. It is a beneficial ingredient to anti-aging compositions since it is quickly absorbed, very hydrating, leaves skin feeling silky soft, and improves skin elasticity.

Read more about identifying the purest Prickly Pear Seed Oil to make the best decision for your skin.


Is Prickly Pear Seed Oil Non-Comedogenic?

It is a common misconception that all facial oils clog your pores. Prickly Pear Seed Oil is a magical oil that has a comedogenic rating of 0. Prickly Pear Seed Oil is fast absorbing which means it seeps straight into your skin and moisturizes it from within. This makes Prickly Pear Seed Oil a great addition to the skincare routine of people who have sensitive or acne-prone skin, as it will never clog your pores!

If you are above the age of 40 and looking to make the switch to a clean and conscious skincare routine, make sure you read our blog on Essential Natural Skincare for Women above 40.


Summing Up

Numerous beneficial compounds with moisturizing, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties can be found in Prickly Pear Seed Oil. The oil's benefits lie in reducing skin inflammation while also avoiding sun damage and acne.

Using products with 100% pure and organic Prickly Pear Seed Oil will help your skin go a long way in its healing and regenerative properties!

Since ancient times, rosehip seed oil has been regarded as the "Queen of Natural Skincare." Rosehip Seed Oil is one of the best oils for menopausal skin, the anti-aging lifesaver you've been looking for. It is praised as the one-stop shop ingredient for anti-aging. It boosts collagen, reduces wrinkles, treats hyperpigmentation, and moisturizes your skin from within.

Rosehip Seed Oil is incredibly light compared to many facial oils. It absorbs into your skin without leaving behind any heavy or oily residue. The perfect ingredient for all things skin-related. Your skin will glow better than ever before!


Why does Rosehip Seed Oil work?

Vitamins, fatty acids, and antioxidants in Rosehip Seed Oil work synergistically to treat mature skin problems.

100% Pure Rosehip Seed Oil also contains Vitamins A, E, and C. These vitamins work as antioxidants to fight free radicals and protect your skin barrier.

Additionally, linoleic acid and other necessary fatty acids found in Rosehip Seed Oil help to maintain the skin's natural cell and tissue turnover, making it radiant from within. Due to its high antioxidant content, rosehip seed oil offers a high level of protection against inflammation.

Rosehip Seed Oil contains the 'natural' form of Vitamin A as opposed to the form found in medical retinoids. As a result, you receive all the advantages of vitamin A without the dry skin side effects that are sometimes seen while using retinoids for the first time.

Benefits of Rosehip Seed Oil for Menopausal Skin

1. Reduces Wrinkles and Fine Lines

Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, Rosehip Seed Oil helps reduce the appearance of Wrinkles and fine lines.

The oil helps increase your cell’s turnover and rejuvenation leaving you with smooth and texture-free skin. It’s no surprise that the oil has long been used for ages as a treatment for healing wounds and to reduce the appearance of scars and fine lines.

2. Tackles Hyperpigmentation

Hyperpigmentation is extremely common in menopause and occurs when an increase in melanin forms dark spots on the skin.

Rosehip Seed Oil is rich in Vitamin A which is made up of several nutritional compounds including lycopene, beta carotene, and retinoids. It makes a multipurpose ingredient to reduce hyperpigmentation, inflammation and other signs of aging with regular use.

3. Boosts Collagen Formation

Collagen is known as the building block of our skin. It is necessary for firmness and flexibility of the skin. As you become older, your body naturally produces less collagen.

Vitamin A, which is essential for the creation of collagen, is abundant in rosehip oil. Rosehip is also demonstrated to stop the body from producing enzymes that destroy collagen making it a must-have for any menopausal skincare routine.

4. Protects Against Sun Damage

Premature aging is largely caused by a lifetime of sun damage. The body's capacity to make collagen can also be hampered by exposure to ultraviolet (UV) light.

Vitamins A and E and other antioxidants, found in rosehip oil, have been demonstrated to work together to prevent visible sun damage. They also could aid in avoiding photoaging, making aging skin look radiant and healthier than ever!

5. Intense Moisture and Hydration

Numerous important fatty acids, such as linoleic and linolenic acid, can be found in Rosehip Seed Oil. Rosehip oil is a fantastic choice for moisturizing dry, irritated skin because it contains essential fatty acids and antioxidants.

Fatty acids support robust cell walls to prevent water loss. Additionally, the oil is quickly absorbed by the skin, allowing antioxidants to penetrate all the way to the skin's deepest layers.

And these are just the gist! Rosehip Seed Oil has so many other benefits that help your skin remain supple and healthy in the long run.

Menopausal FAQs of Rosehip Seed Oil

Q: Does Rosehip Seed Oil Increase Estrogen Levels?

As you enter menopause, estrogen levels tend to decrease. Rose hips are believed to boost estrogen levels due to their rich vitamin C content. Vitamin C may enhance estrogen absorption, impacting individuals taking estrogen supplements.

Furthermore, rose hips contain compounds that stimulate cell renewal, promoting skin rejuvenation. Maintaining adequate vitamin C intake is essential for maintaining proper estrogen levels. Those with vitamin C deficiencies might experience decreased estrogen levels.

Q: Does Rosehip Balance Hormones?

Rosehip seed oil is thought to help balance hormones through its rich nutrient profile. It contains essential fatty acids, such as linoleic acid and oleic acid, which are important in hormone production. Additionally, it's a good source of antioxidants and vitamins, which can support endocrine system health.

More research is needed to solidify these claims. Some believe that the properties of rosehip seed oil may contribute to hormone balance.

Q: Is Rosehip Seed Oil Good for Anti-aging?

Absolutely! It is an incredible ingredient to incorporate into any anti-aging routine. It is a natural source of Vitamin A. It can gently yet effectively help reduce signs of aging. The rich concentration of fatty acids and antioxidants helps your skin look youthful. Moreover, it helps battle most menopausal skin symptoms. These include scars, hyperpigmentation, dryness, and more.

Q: Does Rosehip Boost Collagen?

Yes, rosehip oil is often touted for its potential to boost collagen production in the skin. This is attributed to its high content of vitamin A, which is essential for collagen synthesis.

Collagen is a protein responsible for maintaining skin elasticity and firmness. By providing the skin with vitamin A, rosehip oil may support collagen production and reduce fine lines and wrinkles. Rosehip can help with sagging and loose skin, too.

Rosehip has demonstrated the ability to prevent the formation of MMP-1. MMP-1 is an enzyme responsible for the degradation of collagen within the body.

Q: Is It OK to Use Rosehip Seed Oil Every Day?

Yes, it is generally safe to use rosehip seed oil daily as part of your skincare routine. Rosehip seed oil is known for its nourishing and moisturizing properties, and it can benefit the skin when used regularly. In fact, Rosehip Seed Oil has one of the lowest comedogenic ratings, which means that it will not clog your pores. It is also safe to use during pregnancy, making it one of the safest ingredients.

However, it's essential to patch-test any new skincare product, including rosehip seed oil. This is to ensure you don't have any adverse reactions. Also, consider your skin type and specific needs. While many people find daily use beneficial, others might prefer using it less frequently. This depends on your skin's individual response and any preexisting skincare regimen.

This is why we have used Rosehip Seed Oil as the hero ingredient in all our products. Empress Naturals Serum Set contains Rosehip Seed Oil that can be used in your AM and PM skincare routine.

Q: Can I Use Rosehip Oil Under My Eyes?

Rosehip oil is highly moisturizing and vitamin-rich. As a result, it promotes skin regeneration and protects against puffiness, wrinkles, and dark circles. It is an organic ingredient that is more suited for the under-eyes than other ingredients. Unlike retinoids, it does not contain any synthetic or harsh preservatives that might irritate skin.

Q: Can I Leave Rosehip Oil on My Face Overnight?

Yes, you can. Rosehip Seed Oil has rejuvenating and regenerative properties that make it the perfect nighttime serum. Moreover, it is a dry oil that sinks straight into the skin without any greasy feeling, making it perfect for overnight moisturization.

Empress Naturals AM Face Serum contains Rosehip Seed Oil and boasts incredible renewing properties for the skin.

Q: Does Rosehip Oil Act Like Retinol?

Rosehip seed oil does not contain retinol but does mimic some of its characteristics. It contains beta-carotenes, which help boost collagen production. Moreover, it has a high concentration of Vitamin A and is known for its anti-aging benefits. Benefits like reducing the appearance of signs of aging and improving skin elasticity are also similar to those of retinol.

However, Rosehip Seed Oil is different from retinol in its gentle way of reacting to the skin. It is a more natural form of Vitamin A that may take longer to show results. However, it does not dry out the skin and is safe to use during pregnancy.

Brands To Check Out For Pure And High Quality Rosehip Seed Oil

To ensure that you are purchasing the finest Rosehip Seed Oil, it is essential to choose goods that are USDA Certified Organic, natural, and clean.

But First, Patch Test!

Even though Rosehip Seed Oil is a fantastic addition to any skincare regimen, it's always a good idea to patch-test any new ingredients or products. Put two or three drops of the product behind each ear or on your wrist. You can use the product on your face and skin without risk if there is no eruption within 96 hours of your patch test.
