Prickly Pear Seed Oil, sometimes referred to as ‘Tuna Oil’ or ‘Cactus Fig Oil’ is revered within skincare as one of the most restoring and healing botanicals. We would even go as far as to say that the luxury associated with organic prickly pear seed oil is unmatched. Prickly Pear Seed Oil is no less than royalty in skincare. In fact, its history in the European continent from the Americas is directly linked with royalty.

Prickly Pear Seed Oil is one of the most soothing and hydrating essential oils. Filled with antioxidants and Vitamin E, it is super potent to treat or prevent any skincare concerns you might have. It is not surprising that it has swiftly become one of the most popular oils on the market given its powerful advantages and numerous uses.

Fun Fact - Prickly Pear Seed Oil has one of the highest concentrations of Vitamin E!

In this blog, we’ll talk about :

  • What Makes Prickly Pear Seed Oil Special? We explore its history, rarity, and why it’s considered a luxury in skincare.

  • How to Choose the Best Quality Oil Learn how to identify pure, organic, and cold-pressed Prickly Pear Seed Oil by examining its extraction method, physical characteristics, and price.

  • Skin Benefits & How to Use It Discover the anti-aging, hydrating, and healing properties of this powerful oil, along with tips on incorporating it into your skincare routine.

The Origin of Prickly Pear Seed Oil

Let’s go back centuries- yes, centuries. That’s how long the Prickly Pear Seed has been a superfood for beauty, aesthetics, and healing. At the end of the 15th century, the prickly pear cactus—native to the Americas, especially Mexico, Colombia, and other South American countries—was introduced to Europe. Christopher Columbus is believed to have presented it as a gift to Queen Isabella of Spain.

The plant is said to have adapted incredibly well to the Mediterranean climate of these countries. It has made it a popular cactus to cultivate today in many parts of the world, including Australia, Africa, and the United States.


Extraction and Sustainability of Prickly Pear Seed Oil

While the prickly pear cacti grow in plenty, the process of extracting its oil is what is most intriguing to any skincare enthusiast. The process is extremely time-consuming and costly. This makes this oil one of the rarest and most sought-after organic oils. Only 5% of the fruit's oil can be recovered from the seeds, which need to be hand-picked from the pulp.

Fun Fact - About one million seeds and thirty-six hours of labor are needed to produce one liter of oil.

Yet, prickly pear is said to be one of the most sustainable plants due to its cacti nature. It requires minimum water, and its cultivation does not contribute to high levels of destruction to surrounding biodiversity.


How Can You Choose the Best Prickly Pear Seed Oil?

As a first-time consumer, here are some of the more identifiable ways to distinguish between the various grades and qualities of prickly pear seed oil.

But here’s my #1 rule - before anything check to see if it is organic prickly pear seed oil. If yes, move on to the next steps.

Step 2: Extraction Method

The oil is extracted from the seed of a prickly pear. There are many ways in which different varieties of this oil are extracted - each serving its own purpose and benefit.

We use pure 100% cold-pressed Prickly Pear Seed Oil. Cold-pressed prickly pear seed oil retains the oil's incredible benefits while preserving its natural integrity. Cold-pressing preserves the oil's original color, fragrance, and composition by extracting it without the use of heat or additives.

Step 3: Physical Characteristics

Check the physical characteristics of the Prickly Pear Seed Oil. What does it look and smell like? The best quality Prickly Pear Seed Oils should have the following physical characteristics:

  1. Color: Cold-pressed Prickly Pear Seed Oil of the finest quality can be yellow or green. If the oil looks brown or red, it may have already oxidized or be a subpar product.

  2. Fragrance: Prickly Pear Seed Oil's scent is a key factor in determining whether it is truly pure and natural. Organic Prickly Pear Seed Oil has a rather pleasant smell replicating that of olive or almond oil.

  3. Consistency: 100% pure Prickly Pear Seed Oil is light and effortlessly absorbs into the skin leaving behind no sticky film.

  4. Price: Prickly Due to its process of extraction and the enormous quantity of seeds required to produce every ounce of the oil, prickly pear seed oil is among the most expensive face oils in the world. Products with 100% Pure Prickly Pear Oil can retail for upwards of $40.



Benefits of Prickly Pear Seed Oil for Mature Skin

  1. Anti-aging: Rich in antioxidants, prickly pear is great for wrinkles and combats free radicals. Encouraging the synthesis of collagen, prickly pear seed oil increases the firmness and suppleness of the skin while reducing the visibility of wrinkles and fine lines.

  2. Regenerative Properties: Prickly Pear heals wounds, scars, and skin damage by encouraging the skin's cells to regenerate. It promotes a smoother, younger-looking complexion by quickening the skin's natural regeneration process.

  3. Brightening of the Skin: has inherent skin-brightening qualities. It increases luminosity, evens skin tone, and reduces the visibility of hyperpigmentation evident in menopause.

  4. Healing and Calming: It relieves irritated and inflamed skin because of its anti-inflammatory qualities. Because it facilitates the healing process, it is advantageous for rosacea, eczema, and acne.

  5. Hydrating and Moisturising: Very hydrating, supporting the skin and hair's hydration and nourishment. Prickly pear seed oil prevents moisture loss and leaves the skin and hair feeling smooth and soft.

  6. Antioxidant-rich: Abundant in antioxidants, it protects the skin from environmental stresses, scavenging free radicals and fostering a more youthful look. Additionally, it promotes a bright, rejuvenated complexion, decreases oxidative damage, and increases skin resilience.

  7. Absorbs Straightaway: Such a boon for mature skin because prickly pear absorbs into the skin quickly and doesn't leave a greasy aftereffect. It is lightweight, guarantees quick penetration, and provides vital minerals and moisture.

This is not an exhaustive list. We’ve highlighted the Prickly Pear Seed Oil benefits in detail in our dedicated blog.

How To Use Prickly Pear Seed Oil

  • Use the facial oil twice daily on freshly cleansed skin.

  • Simply dispense a few drops, warm them in your hands, and gently press them onto your face.

  • For added moisture, you can also apply any excess oil on your hands to the tips of your hair.

We highly recommend trying Empress Naturals 100% Pure Prickly Pear Seed Oil, which contains cold-pressed organic Prickly Pear Seed Oil. When it comes to menopause-specific skincare, Prickly Pear Seed Oil can be helpful with combating acne, wrinkles, hydration, and dark spots.

For more detailed information on using Prickly Pear Seed Oil, you can refer to our dedicated blog.


Why choose Empress Naturals?

It is imperative to pick Prickly Pear Seed Oil products that are Certified Organic, natural, and clean so you know that you are getting the purest product.

It's important to choose conscious skincare products that are truly 100% natural and have ingredient lists that are simple to comprehend. Your skin is unique, and keeping it young, elastic, and radiant calls for talent and purity!


Key Takeaways

1. How can I tell if Prickly Pear Seed Oil is high quality?

Look for cold-pressed, organic oil with a light texture, yellow-green color, and a mild, nutty scent similar to olive or almond oil. Avoid products that appear brown, red, or overly thick, as they may be oxidized or impure.

2. What are the main skincare benefits of Prickly Pear Seed Oil?

This oil is packed with antioxidants and Vitamin E, making it a powerhouse for hydration, anti-aging, skin brightening, and healing. It absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy residue, making it ideal for all skin types, especially mature skin.

3. How should I use Prickly Pear Seed Oil in my skincare routine?

Apply a few drops to clean skin morning and night, pressing it gently into your face. It pairs well with other skincare ingredients like Rosehip Oil and Vitamin C for enhanced hydration and radiance.

posts.title: Our Top Tips to Help You Balance Motherhood and Skincare

posts.content:
First of all, congratulations mom! Pregnancy is definitely full of its ups and downs. And so are the skincare problems after pregnancy!

Enjoy the joy and chaos of caring for your little one. But it also becomes easy to neglect self-care, especially when it comes to skincare. We understand that as a busy mom, finding time for yourself can be a luxury. However, taking care of your skin is not only about looking good; it's about feeling good too.

In this blog, we'll explore how your skin changes during and after pregnancy. We also look at the common skincare problems you may encounter, and the best ways to take care of your skin amidst the whirlwind of motherhood. From avoiding harmful chemicals in skincare products to perfecting your nighttime routine, we've got you covered. Plus, we'll delve into the power of nutrition and how a well-balanced diet can enhance your skin's health and radiance.

Our goal is to empower you with knowledge and self-care tips that allow you to embrace motherhood while looking and feeling your best. Join us on this journey as we celebrate the beauty of motherhood and the art of nurturing your skin with natural and organic solutions.


How Does Your Skin Change Post-Partum?

It’s no secret that pregnancy can cause havoc on your hormones and your skin! Throughout pregnancy, your skin faces heightened demands as it expands, not just in the belly but also in other areas. It responds to hormonal, blood flow, and glandular changes.

After delivery, your body undergoes adjustments, which may take time. During this transition, your skin might seem to fluctuate between extremes before finding its new normal. This emphasizes the significance of postpartum skincare.

Here are some changes you might notice in your skin postpartum:

  • Melasma: Hormonal fluctuations can lead to darkened patches. Known as melasma they affect 50% of pregnancies in the second trimester.
  • Acne: Increased progesterone causes heightened oiliness and acne in the first trimester. However, it often improves in the second trimester.
  • Spider Veins: The rise in blood volume can result in spider veins on pregnant women's lower legs. It often reverses postpartum. Compression socks can help prevent their occurrence.
  • Stretch Marks: Expanding skin areas like the breasts and belly can develop stretch marks, regardless of oil application

Moreover, your skin type can change which can result in skin problems after pregancy. These new found problems can be harder to deal with if you don't have the right guidance.

Postpartum Acne

After giving birth, some women may experience postpartum acne. This occurs due to fluctuations in hormones which stimulate increased oil production. The excess oil can clog pores, leading to the formation of acne. Stress and lack of sleep during the postpartum period can also contribute to acne breakouts.

Postpartum Oily Skin

Postpartum hormonal fluctuations can lead to ongoing issues with oily skin. The surge of hormones during pregnancy can cause an overproduction of sebum, leading to greasy and shiny skin even after delivery. This excess oil can contribute to clogged pores and the formation of acne.

Postpartum Dry Skin

On the other hand, some women may experience postpartum dry skin. Hormonal changes, along with the stress of childbirth and changes in weather conditions, can lead to a decrease in skin's natural moisture retention. As a result, the skin may feel tight, rough, and flaky.

Postpartum Stretch Marks

Stretch marks are a common skin problem after pregnancy, especially in areas like the abdomen, breasts, thighs, and hips. The rapid stretching of the skin during pregnancy causes the elastic fibers to break down, resulting in stretch marks. These marks may appear as reddish or purple streaks initially and gradually fade to a silvery color over time

But, you don't have to worry! We have out together a list of the best tips that wil help you take care of yourself and your skin during this new phase of motherhood!

How to Take Care of Your Post-Partum Skin

Avoid Harmful Chemical-Laden Skincare

Avoiding chemicals is not only for skincare problems after pregnancy. There are so many harmful chemicals in your daily skincare. They could be causing problems you are unaware of.

Prioritizing organic, sustainable, and chemical-free products is very beneficial. After childbirth, your body is still adjusting, and the skin may be sensitive. Opt for skincare products with gentle and organic ingredients. It helps reduce the risk of irritation or allergic reactions.

Look for plant-based extracts, nourishing oils, and soothing botanicals. Steer clear of harsh chemicals like parabens, sulfates, and synthetic fragrances. A gentle and toxin-free approach will help your skin recover. No need for unnecessary stress and you can enhance the well-being of both you and your baby.

Find Multipurpose Ingredients

As a new mother, time is precious. Streamlining your skincare routine with multipurpose ingredients can be a game-changer. Look for products that offer many benefits. For eg. a moisturizer with added sun protection or a nourishing oil that can be used for both face and body.

This not only saves time but also simplifies your skincare regimen, making it more manageable amidst your busy schedule. Investing in multitasking products allows you to care for your skin while juggling the demands of motherhood.

Consider the amazing benefits of Rosehip Seed Oil. It offers excellent hydration, nourishment, and potent anti-aging benefits. Selecting the appropriate Rosehip Seed Oil ensures you receive exceptional results and top-notch quality for your skincare needs.

Prickly Pear Seed Oil is also an incredible multi-tasker for your skin and hair!

Perfect your Nighttime Routine

A well-crafted nighttime skincare routine is crucial for postpartum skin recovery. During sleep, the skin undergoes significant repair and regeneration. This is an ideal time to nourish and replenish.

Start with a gentle cleanser to remove makeup and impurities. Follow up with a hydrating serum or oil to lock in moisture. You need skin-repairing ingredients like Rosehip Seed Oil, Geranium Oil, and Vitamin E.

Keep in mind that perfecting your nighttime routine doesn't mean it needs to be 8 steps long! Keep it minimal and find a product that does the job in 1-2 steps.

Our Luxe Night Elixir, for instance, is the perfect amalgamation of botanicals. It repairs, replenishes and hydrates! Moreover, it is literally perfect for any skin type, making it perfect for all your skincare problems after pregnancy.

Prioritise Organisation

An organized setup means a consistent skincare routine. There is nothing as important as organization for new moms. Maintaining visible and organized skincare proves essential for time efficiency.

As a busy mom, you can seamlessly follow the necessary steps. No more wasting precious moments. Establish a structured regimen that works for your skin. It will address your specific concerns and prevent future issues. Organised skincare will definitely mean reducing any added stress.

Easy access to products during rushed moments is facilitated through organization. You can ensure to find what you need to take care of your skin.

Balanced Diet

For new moms, nutrition plays a crucial role in both physical health and mental well-being. The demands of juggling various responsibilities can be physically and emotionally taxing. This makes a balanced diet even more important.

Taking care of your skin starts from within. Nourishing your body with nutrients can have a positive impact on your skin's health.

Drinking plenty of water to stay hydrated is vital for maintaining skin elasticity. Including foods with vitamins A, C, and E can help combat skin damage. It can also help boost collagen production. Prioritize colorful fruits and vegetables for their skin-loving antioxidants.

But let's pause and reflect on this: when we neglect our own needs, we aren't doing anyone any favors. Instead, we find ourselves exhausted, stressed, and burnt out. And guess what? That directly impacts our ability to be the best parents we can be.

posts.excerpt: Adopting sustainability practices into our everyday lives has become a huge topic of conversation in recent years, given the concerning state of the environment. It...
posts.content:
Adopting sustainability practices into our everyday lives has become a huge topic of conversation in recent years, given the concerning state of the environment.

It has become imperative that we all do our best to reduce the harm we each cause to the health of the environment, and the skincare that we use is no exception. More and more brands are flipping the script and adhering to the cultural moment and doing things to make their products be more eco-friendly.

Here are a few reasons why this may be a good decision for you to make as well, and the best way of going about it.

Why the Skincare Industry is Gravitating Towards Sustainability

Now more than ever, consumers are abandoning brands that they love and have been loyal to because they would rather spend their money on brands that are committed to being green and friendlier to the environment.

A blog on the popular skincare site Arcona mentioned a recent study found that 72% of consumers are actively picking out environmentally friendly products.

Sustainability is amongst the biggest trends happening within so many industries including the beauty industry, and brands that haven’t done anything to make a change in their harmful practices (such as eliminating single-use plastics) are set to be left in the past.

When looking at sustainability through the lens of beauty and skincare, it can mean a lot of things. Many brands are reconstructing their packaging to be reusable and/or recyclable, which is a huge step in the right direction of eliminating single use plastics and making them obsolete.

posts.excerpt: Aging is a natural process, but no one wants to look older than they feel. During menopause and perimenopause, many women seek skincare solutions that...
posts.content:
Aging is a natural process, but no one wants to look older than they feel. During menopause and perimenopause, many women seek skincare solutions that can restore youthful radiance, a healthy glow, and firm, supple skin despite hormonal changes.

Can you STOP aging? The simple answer is no. But can you prevent and minimize visible signs of aging during menopause and perimenopause? Absolutely! By incorporating natural anti-aging facial oils and making strategic lifestyle adjustments, your menopausal skin can truly look ageless.

Bonus tip: By making minute lifestyle and routine changes, your skin can truly look AGELESS!


WHY DOES YOUR SKIN AGE?

Your skin ages due to a combination of environmental and inherent factors that often work simultaneously towards the breaking down of proteins and molecules in your body. Your genes in association with lifestyle choices and the environment will play a massive role in the outward appearance of signs of aging like wrinkles, fine lines, and sun damage.

Why Anti-Aging Facial Oils Are Essential for Menopausal Skin

Natural facial oils become crucial during menopause because they replenish the skin's lipid barrier that deteriorates with declining estrogen. Unlike water-based products, oils penetrate deeply to deliver fatty acids, antioxidants, and vitamins that support collagen production and skin repair—exactly what menopausal skin needs most.

Loss of Collagen

Like all organs in your body, skin is held together by molecules that bind its composing tissues and cells together. This molecule is known as Collagen.

Collagen is a type of protein that is present in your skin, muscles, bones, ligaments, and tendons. It accounts for around 30% to 40% of all proteins in our body. It is a component of our connective tissues, which is accountable for the firmness and structure of the body. Collagen is essential for the overall health of your body, but it is especially critical for the suppleness of your skin.

It fortifies your skin and improves its elasticity and moisture. Collagen fibers provide the framework for elastin and hyaluronic acid, which are essential for the suppleness and hydration of the skin.

As you age, your body generates less collagen. The skin thins, gets drier, and loses elasticity, resulting in the exaggeration of visible wrinkles on your face and body.

The loss of Collagen in your body can be externally noticed if you observe an increase in joint pains, sagging skin, an increase in wrinkles and fine lines, and loss of hair.


Environmental Exposure aka Sun Damage

We've all heard that excessive sun exposure can accelerate skin aging, but how does this happen? Research has found that Ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun affects specific fibers in the skin called elastin. Over time, these fibers are unable to return to their maximum performance capabilities which results in what we refer to as sun damage or photoaging.

The loss of elastin fibers causes the skin to droop, wrinkle, and lose its elasticity to bounce back after stretching.

The skin also bruises and rips more easily, with a slower healing process. So, while sun damage may not be visible while you're young, it becomes more apparent later in life.

While nothing can totally reverse sun damage, sometimes skin can recover if it is taken care of appropriately. It is never too late to start shielding yourself from the sun by incorporating the right kind of sun barrier products in your skincare routine.

Repairing Sun Damage with Facial Oils

While nothing can completely reverse sun damage, menopausal skin can recover significantly with appropriate care. Natural anti-aging facial oils rich in antioxidants, vitamins A and C, and essential fatty acids help repair photoaged skin by supporting cellular regeneration and barrier function.

It's never too late to start protecting and repairing your skin during perimenopause and menopause by incorporating targeted facial oils and proper sun protection in your skincare routine.

Lifestyle Choices

Your lifestyle choices i.e. the way you live your life can also affect how signs of aging develop on your skin.

  • Lack of an Appropriate Skincare Routine: Different types of skin have different needs that need to be met to maintain the skin’s natural composition. For instance, dry skin types require extra hydration through topical skincare products that help the skin stay healthy and hydrated.

    Moreover, as mentioned earlier, sun protection is also a crucial step in any skincare routine without which the skin is vulnerable to sun damage.

  • Sleeping Patterns and Position: A regular lack of sleep or even the way you sleep can contribute to the exaggeration of wrinkles and fine lines on your face. When your body does not get the rest it requires, your skin is unable to restore and rejuvenate, leading to dull and tired skin. How you sleep might also have an impact.

    Sleep creases are most typically observed on the sides of your forehead, between the eyebrows, the hairline, as well as in the center of the cheeks. These are caused by how your face is oriented on the pillow and could become more noticeable as the skin loses its flexibility.

  • Smoking or Consumption of Alcohol: People who smoke or drink heavily have been seen to have more wrinkles than others of the same age, complexion, and sun exposure history.

    In fact, a     study has found the ratio of wrinkles to be 4.7 times greater for those who are regular smokers. While the exact origin of this discrepancy is unknown, these lifestyle choices may interfere with normal blood circulation in the skin.


Best Natural Anti-Aging Facial Oils for Menopause and Perimenopause Skin

Synthetic ingredients may reduce aging signs, but natural facial oils provide powerful anti-aging effects without exposure to hazardous chemicals and preservatives—crucial considerations for hormonally sensitive menopausal skin.

Modern scientific advances have elevated natural facial oils to extraordinary effectiveness, making them the gold standard for menopause and perimenopause skincare.

Rosehip Seed Oil: The Queen of Menopausal Skincare

Why Rosehip Oil Is Essential for Hormonal Skin

Aptly known as the "Queen of Natural Skincare," Rosehip Seed Oil is a miracle anti-aging facial oil that addresses virtually every menopausal skin concern—from hyperpigmentation and wrinkles to sun damage and extreme dryness.

Clinical studies show that after eight weeks of regular use, Rosehip Seed Oil demonstrates remarkable improvement in crow's feet reduction, skin hydration levels, skin elasticity and flexibility, tone and texture, and overall radiance.

The Science Behind Rosehip for Menopause

Rosehip's extraordinary benefits stem from its high concentration of essential fatty acids (including omega-3 and omega-6), vitamin A (natural retinoids), vitamin C, and powerful antioxidants that combat free radicals and inflammation.

For menopausal skin specifically, Rosehip Oil replenishes the lipid barrier compromised by declining estrogen, stimulates collagen production to counteract hormonal loss, reduces hyperpigmentation that worsens during menopause, and improves skin texture and firmness.

This is why we regard Rosehip Seed Oil as our Hero Ingredient for menopause and perimenopause skincare.

Coconut Oil: Deep Hydration for Dry Menopausal Skin

Coconut oil offers antimicrobial and antifungal properties alongside impressive anti-aging and healing benefits—particularly valuable for menopausal skin prone to sensitivity and compromised barrier function.

Virgin coconut oil specifically contains properties that encourage collagen production, helping counteract the dramatic collagen loss during menopause. It's also an exceptionally powerful moisturizer, ideal for the severe dryness many women experience during perimenopause and menopause.

Find it in: Empress Naturals' Luxe Night Elixir for intensive overnight hydration of menopausal skin.

Green Tea Extract: Antioxidant Protection for Aging Skin

Green tea needs no introduction in natural wellness. Its numerous advantages include improved overall health and remarkable skincare benefits, especially for menopausal skin facing increased oxidative stress.

Green tea provides potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that protect hormonal skin from environmental damage, prevent UV damage (critical for vulnerable menopausal skin), reduce collagen breakdown accelerated by menopause, and may protect against skin cancer.

Vitamin E Oil: Circulation and Repair for Menopausal Skin

Vitamin E is abundant in antioxidants and improves blood circulation—essential for menopausal skin that often experiences reduced microcirculation due to hormonal changes.

This makes Vitamin E an excellent solution for wrinkles and photoaging prevalent during menopause and perimenopause. Many women notice significant improvement in skin texture and integrity after applying Vitamin E-rich facial oils topically, as it supports the skin's natural repair processes and protects against free radical damage.

Argan Oil: Liquid Gold for Mature Skin

Often called "liquid gold," Argan oil is exceptionally rich in vitamin E, essential fatty acids, and antioxidants. It's lightweight yet deeply nourishing—perfect for menopausal skin that needs intensive moisture without heaviness.

Argan oil improves skin elasticity, reduces inflammation, repairs the skin barrier, and provides anti-aging benefits through its unique composition of oleic and linoleic acids.

Prickly Pear Seed Oil is one of the most precious facial oils, containing the highest concentration of vitamin E of any beauty oil (150% more than Argan oil) and remarkable levels of vitamin K for brightening and reducing dark circles.

Carrot Seed Oil: Underrated Powerhouse for Aging Skin

Carrot seed oil may not immediately come to mind as a leading anti-aging ingredient, but it absolutely deserves recognition for menopausal skincare. Each oil can be used alone or layered for customized treatment of your specific menopausal skin concerns.

posts.title: Perimenopause Weight Loss: Smart, Science-Backed Strategies
posts.excerpt: Navigating perimenopause weight loss can feel overwhelming, but understanding the science behind your changing body empowers you to take control. At Empress Naturals, we believe...
posts.content:
Navigating perimenopause weight loss can feel overwhelming, but understanding the science behind your changing body empowers you to take control. At Empress Naturals, we believe that evidence-based strategies combined with compassionate self-care create the foundation for successful weight management during this transformative phase of life.

What can you expect:

  • Understanding perimenopause: The 4-10 year hormonal transition affecting 70% of women with weight changes

  • Science behind weight gain: How declining estrogen, slower metabolism, and insulin resistance drive abdominal fat storage

  • Evidence-based strategies: Proven nutrition, exercise, sleep, and stress management techniques for successful perimenopause weight loss

What is Perimenopause?

Perimenopause, literally meaning "around menopause," represents the transitional period leading up to menopause when your reproductive hormones begin their gradual decline. This phase typically begins in your 40s, though it can start as early as your mid-30s or as late as your early 50s, lasting anywhere from four to ten years before your final menstrual period.

What happens during Perimenopause?

During perimenopause, your ovaries gradually produce less estrogen and progesterone, but this decline isn't linear or predictable. Instead, hormone levels fluctuate dramatically from month to month and even within individual cycles. These erratic hormonal swings create the characteristic symptoms of perimenopause:

  1. irregular periods

  2. hot flashes

  3. sleep disturbances

  4. mood changes

  5. and notably changes in body weight and composition.


Perimenopause weight gain

Perimenopause weight gain affects approximately 70% of women during this transition. The average weight gain ranges from 5-10 pounds, but more concerning than the number on the scale is the shift in where your body stores fat. The protective lower-body fat distribution of your reproductive years gives way to increased abdominal fat storage, creating health risks beyond cosmetic concerns.

What makes perimenopause particularly challenging for weight management is the unpredictability of symptoms! One month, you might experience regular cycles with minimal symptoms, while the next brings irregular bleeding, intense hot flashes, and rapid weight fluctuations. This hormonal chaos affects not just your physical body but also your emotional relationship with food, exercise, and body image.

The duration and intensity of perimenopause vary significantly among women, influenced by factors including genetics, lifestyle, stress levels, and overall health status. Recognizing the signs of perimenopause early allows you to implement proactive strategies for managing weight changes before they become entrenched patterns.

The key to successful perimenopause weight loss lies in working with your changing biology rather than against it, using science-based approaches that address the root causes of weight gain during this transitional period.


Why Does Weight Change Happen During Perimenopause?

Estrogen Fluctuations and Fat Storage

  • Declining estrogen shifts fat distribution to the abdominal area during perimenopause

  • Research shows that decreased estrogen increases lipoprotein lipase activity, promoting belly fat storage

  • Visceral fat produces inflammatory compounds that increase insulin resistance and cardiovascular risk

Metabolic Rate Changes

  • Basal metabolic rate naturally decreases with age, accelerated during perimenopause

  • Declining muscle mass (accelerated by estrogen loss) further reduces metabolic rate

  • This creates a cycle where muscle loss leads to slower metabolism and continued weight gain

Insulin Sensitivity and Blood Sugar Regulation

  • Hormonal fluctuations reduce insulin sensitivity during perimenopause

  • Insulin resistance particularly increases abdominal fat accumulation

  • Can trigger increased cravings for carbohydrate-rich foods

Sleep Disruption and Appetite Hormones

  • Hot flashes and night sweats frequently disrupt sleep quality

  • Poor sleep affects hunger hormones: ghrelin (increases appetite) and leptin (signals fullness)

  • Sleep disturbances lead to increased appetite and high-calorie food cravings

  • Research confirms that sleep problems significantly contribute to perimenopause weight loss difficulty

If you want to know more, read our blog on experiencing perimenopause weight gain and changes in menopause body shape. But if you’re here to know what to do to manage this perimenopause sudden weight gain, we’re going to talk about some science and expert-backed tips.


Holistic Tips to Manage Body Weight During Perimenopause

Successful perimenopause weight loss requires a comprehensive approach that addresses the complex hormonal, metabolic, and lifestyle factors contributing to weight changes during this transition.

Nutrition for Hormonal Balance

  1. Protein Prioritization for Metabolic Support: Increasing protein intake becomes crucial during perimenopause to preserve muscle mass and support metabolic rate. Perimenopausal women need 1.2-1.6 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight daily, significantly higher than general recommendations. Include lean meats, fish, eggs, Greek yogurt, legumes, and plant-based proteins like quinoa and hemp seeds.

  2. Timing and Meal Frequency: Consider time-restricted eating or intermittent fasting approaches, which research suggests may help improve insulin sensitivity and support perimenopause weight loss.

  3. Anti-Inflammatory Foods: Include fatty fish rich in omega-3 fatty acids at least twice weekly, colorful vegetables packed with antioxidants, berries, nuts, seeds, and spices like turmeric and ginger. These foods help reduce inflammatory markers that can contribute to insulin resistance and weight gain.

  4. Phytoestrogen Integration: Incorporate foods containing natural plant estrogens that may help balance hormonal fluctuations. Soy products, flaxseeds, chia seeds, and legumes provide gentle estrogenic activity that can help smooth the hormonal rollercoaster of perimenopause while supporting weight management efforts.

Exercise Strategies for Perimenopausal Bodies

  1. Strength Training as Foundation: Resistance training becomes non-negotiable during perimenopause for weight loss, to combat muscle loss and maintain metabolic rate. Strength training can significantly preserve muscle mass and bone density in perimenopausal women. Aim for 2-3 strength training sessions weekly, focusing on compound movements that work multiple muscle groups simultaneously. Start slow and progress accordingly - gradually increasing weight, reps, or sets—ensures continued adaptation and metabolic benefits.

  2. High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT): HIIT workouts can be particularly effective for perimenopause weight loss, helping boost metabolism and improve insulin sensitivity. Examples include cycling intervals, bodyweight circuits, or treadmill sprints.

Sleep Optimization for Hormonal Health

  1. Creating a Sleep Sanctuary: Poor sleep quality during perimenopause can sabotage weight loss efforts by disrupting appetite-regulating hormones. Create an optimal sleep environment by keeping your bedroom cool (65-68°F), dark, and quiet. Use blackout curtains, eye masks, or white noise machines as needed. Invest in moisture-wicking pajamas and breathable bedding to manage night sweats.

  2. Evening Routine Development: Establish a consistent pre-sleep routine to signal your body it's time to wind down. Avoid screens for at least one hour before bed, as blue light can disrupt melatonin production (you can also change the settings on your phone to change the blue light settings!). Instead, try gentle stretching, reading, meditation, or taking a cool shower to lower body temperature before bed.

Stress Management and Cortisol Regulation

  1. Mindfulness-Based Approaches: Chronic stress promotes abdominal fat storage and making perimenopause weight loss more challenging. Practicing mindfulness meditation, deep breathing exercises, or progressive muscle relaxation daily can help reduce menopausal stress.

  2. Adaptogenic Support: Consider adaptogenic herbs like ashwagandha, rhodiola, or holy basil, which may help your body manage stress more effectively. These herbs can help regulate cortisol levels and support overall resilience during the perimenopausal transition. Always work with qualified practitioners when incorporating herbal supplements.

Targeted Supplementation

  1. Essential Nutrients for Metabolic Support: Certain supplements may provide additional support for perimenopause weight loss when combined with lifestyle modifications. Vitamin D and magnesium are crucial for metabolic function and are commonly deficient in perimenopausal women. Omega-3 fatty acids support anti-inflammatory processes and may help with mood regulation and insulin sensitivity.

  2. Probiotic Support: Emerging research suggests that gut health plays a role in weight management and hormonal balance. Consider a high-quality probiotic supplement containing diverse strains, particularly Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium species, which may support healthy weight management and reduce inflammation.

Please consult your healthcare professional before adding supplements to your diet so you get the most out of what your body needs!

Tracking and Monitoring

  1. Beyond the Scale: Focus on measurements that reflect true health improvements rather than just weight loss. Take progress photos and note improvements in how clothes fit, as these often reflect positive changes before the scale moves.

  2. Symptom Tracking: Keep a journal tracking symptoms, sleep quality, stress levels, and eating patterns to identify connections between lifestyle factors and weight fluctuations. This information can help you and your healthcare providers optimize your approach to perimenopause weight loss.

Community Connection

Connect with other women experiencing similar challenges through support groups, online communities, or local fitness classes designed for midlife women. Sharing experiences and strategies can provide motivation, practical tips, and emotional support during this transition.


In Summary

Remember that perimenopause weight loss is typically slower and requires more patience than weight loss during younger years. Focus on building sustainable habits that support your overall health and well-being rather than pursuing quick fixes. The strategies that work during perimenopause often become the foundation for lifelong health and vitality.

posts.title: Experience Blissful Rejuvenation: A Guide to Abhyanga Self-Massages
posts.excerpt: Abhyanga massage, commonly known as Ayurvedic massage is an intentional oil massage practice that is integral to self-care practices. Abhyanga contradicts the idea that oil...
posts.content:
Abhyanga massage, commonly known as Ayurvedic massage is an intentional oil massage practice that is integral to self-care practices. Abhyanga contradicts the idea that oil massages are something solely reserved for vacations, birthdays, or special occasions. Instead, Abhyanga massage is a practice of self-massage recommended in daily self-care practice, especially for those with symptoms of perimenopause and menopause including dry skin, insomnia, anxiety, and stress.

Abhyanga massage, which is great for healing and pain reduction, is typically distinguished by a certain set of strokes, such as lengthy, sweeping motions, and generous amounts of warm, herbal oils that are appropriate for the individual. Once you've mastered the fundamentals, self-massage becomes second nature and so worth the effort.


In this blog, we will cover:

  • What is Abhyanga and its origin?

  • The benefits of abhyanga

  • A step-by-step process of how to start your own abhyanga practice


What is Abhyanga Massage?

Abhyanga is an old Ayurvedic practice that includes applying heated oil to the body and then massaging your body. The term abhyanga is said to be derived from the Sanskrit terms anga (movement) and abhi (different, against, or opposed). Abhyanga massage is also a part of the ‘Snehana’ practice which translates to ‘with love and nature’. This makes sense since it is a practice that focuses on self-care wherein you carefully and gently massage ayurvedic oils into your skin.


The Indian Origin of Abhyanga Oil Massage

Abhyanga practice was originally referenced in the approximately 2,000-year-old Ayurvedic classic Charaka Samhita. According to the scripture, abhyanga has many benefits as a regular practice that can aid enhance circulation, immunity, and general wellness.

Abhyanga became an important aspect of Ayurvedic therapy over time, and it was utilized to address a variety of health problems. According to the previously stated published review, abhyanga is traditionally a part of daily practices, for preserving health or as a component of the pretreatment technique focused on a more rigorous "detox" therapy regimen.



Benefits of Abhyanga Oil Massage

  • Improves Circulation: Enhances lymphatic flow and blood circulation, aiding in toxin removal and healing.

  • Relieves Stress and Anxiety: Calms the mind, reduces tension, and relaxes the nervous system with warm oil massage.

  • Improves Sleep Quality: Encourages deeper sleep by balancing energy and easing insomnia.

  • Promotes Joint and Muscle Health: Loosens tight muscles, increases flexibility, reduces inflammation, and supports healing.

  • Harmonizes the Doshas: Restores balance in Vata, Pitta, and Kapha by using dosha-specific oils.

  • Enhances Emotional Well-Being: Releases endorphins, lifts mood, reduces exhaustion, and fosters inner peace.

Our dedicated blog on the benefits of abhyanga goes into detail about the ways in which abhyanga is incredible for your body, mind, and soul.



How to do Abyangha Massage

Doing Abhyanga is fairly simple once you have figured out the oil to use and techniques.

Step 1: Identify your Dosha

Abyanga is thought to balance the doshas - the three basic energies that regulate the physical and mental activities of the body. The three doshas are - vata, pitta, and kapha.

  1. Vata (Air & Ether): Governs movement and creativity. Vata types are slim, energetic, and imaginative but prone to anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.

  2. Pitta (Fire & Water): Controls metabolism and transformation. Pitta individuals are driven, passionate, and of medium build but may experience irritability, inflammation, and skin issues.

  3. Kapha (Earth & Water): Provides structure and stability. Kapha types are sturdy, calm, and compassionate but may struggle with weight gain, fatigue, and congestion.

An easy way to identify your dosha can be to take a free online Dosha quiz.


Step 2: Picking the Best Oils for Abhyanga according to your Dosha

Picking the right massage oil for you based on your dosha is important to bring balance between your mind and soul.


Oils for Vata: Sesame oil, which is warming and nourishing, is one of the greatest oils for balancing Vata dosha. It aids in the lubrication of joints, the improvement of circulation, and the relaxation of the neural system. Another excellent choice is almond oil, because of its high vitamin E concentration that helps to nourish and moisturize the skin. Avocado oil is also a fantastic option since it contains an abundance of healthy fats that nourish and preserve the skin.

Abhyanga massage oil recipe for Vata Dosha:

Ingredients:

1/2 cup of organic sesame oil

10 drops of lavender essential oil

10 drops of sweet orange essential oil

5 drops of frankincense essential oil

Combine oils and bring to a warm yet comfortable temperature before use



Oils for Pitta: Coconut oil is one of the greatest oils for Pitta dosha balance since it is cooling and calming. It nourishes and moisturizes the skin while also having anti-inflammatory effects that assist in decreasing redness and inflammation. Sunflower oil is another excellent choice since it contains linoleic acid, a fatty acid which helps to enhance the skin's barrier function and decrease irritation. Jojoba oil is also a wonderful option because it is non-comedogenic and aids in the regulation of oil .

Abhyanga massage oil recipe for Pitta Dosha:

Ingredients:

1/2 cup of organic coconut oil

10 drops of rose essential oil

5 drops of sandalwood essential oil

5 drops of peppermint essential oil

Combine oils and bring to a warm yet comfortable temperature before use




Oils for Kapha: Mustard oil, which is hot and invigorating, is one of the greatest oils for balancing Kapha dosha. It aids in the improvement of circulation, digestion, and the removal of toxins from the body. Ginger oil is another excellent choice since it is both warming and revitalizing. It aids in the improvement of circulation, reducing inflammation, and detoxification of the body. Eucalyptus oil is also invigorating and energizing. It aids in the removal of congestion, the improvement of respiratory function, and the promotion of mental clarity.

Abhyanga massage oil recipe for Kapha Dosha:

Ingredients:

1/2 cup of organic almond oil

10 drops of eucalyptus essential oil

5 drops of ginger essential oil

5 drops of cinnamon essential oil

Combine oils and bring to a warm yet comfortable temperature before use




Step 3: Abhyanga Massage Technique

Abhyanga does not need to be a daily full-body massage. Instead, you can pick a few parts of your body that you want to focus on your massage technique. We've also perfected the self-massage routine for all your restorative needs.

Empress Tip: The most important thing is that abhyanga is performed with presence and doesn't turn into a shallow oil application; instead, you should truly feel that part of your body.

    • Belly & Chest Abhyanga Self-Massage: To truly feel the effects of oil, apply it to the belly in a clockwise, motion, beginning at the center of the navel and gradually expanding the circles and pressure depth. Then, using a flat palm on one hand, massage from the lower hip or belly over the chest or heart and over the shoulder.

    • Neck Abhyanga Self-Massage: Press your index and middle fingers at the base of your neck and slowly move upward toward your scalp.

    • Middle Trapezius Shoulder Self-Massage: Apply pressure where your neck meets your shoulders for 10–30 seconds, repeating as needed.

    • Upper Trapezius Shoulder Self-Massage: With your arm bent, massage from your shoulder inward toward your neck, repeating until the tension eases.

    • Feet and Leg Abhyanga Self-Massage: begin by crossing one foot over your other, stretched, and massage it, including the soles and ankles. You have the option to alternate or massage the second foot in the same manner, massaging the entire foot and leg. Use lengthy strokes to massage the lower leg and calf, rounder, softer strokes to massage the knee, and long strokes to massage the thighs, including the inner and outer thighs and hips.


    Key Tips to Remember

    • Massage each area for 30 seconds to 1 minute to prevent soreness.

    • Be gentle and listen to your body to avoid injury.

    • Warm oil enhances relaxation and absorption, so apply it generously.

    • Repeat wherever necessary.


    Frequently Asked Questions

    1. How do I choose the right oil for my dosha?

    • Vata: Sesame, almond, or avocado oil for warmth and nourishment.

    • Pitta: Coconut, sunflower, or jojoba oil for cooling and calming.

    • Kapha: Mustard, ginger, or eucalyptus oil for stimulation and detoxification.

    2. Can I massage only specific areas instead of my whole body?

    Yes! Focus on high-tension areas like the head, neck, shoulders, or feet if you don’t have time for a full-body massage.

    3. How does Abhyanga help with perimenopause and menopause?

    It helps alleviate symptoms like dry skin, insomnia, anxiety, and stress by deeply nourishing the body and calming the nervous system.

    4. When Should I Avoid Abhyanga?

    You should avoid Abhyanga if you have a fever, cold, flu, or any active infection, as massage can spread toxins and worsen symptoms. It’s also best to skip it during the first few days of menstruation, after eating, or if you have open wounds, severe inflammation, or a serious medical condition.

Clean Skincare to get Rid of Visible Signs of Aging

Smiling. Frowning. Laughing. Crying. Every facial expression you make throughout your life contributes to your skin's texture and appearance as you age—especially during menopause when declining estrogen accelerates wrinkle formation dramatically. But does that mean you should stop laughing when something is funny or stop expressing emotions? That's not possible, nor should it be.

Wrinkles, fine lines, and sagging skin are natural parts of aging, but they become significantly more pronounced during perimenopause and menopause due to hormonal changes. The good news? There are natural, effective ways to reduce their appearance with the right ingredients, care, and routine specifically designed for menopausal skin.

In this blog, we'll explore how natural ingredients can combat wrinkles during menopause and show you how Empress Naturals' clean, organic formulations provide maximum anti-aging benefits for hormonally changing skin.

Dealing with Wrinkles, Fine Lines, and Sagging Skin in Menopause

Why Menopause Accelerates Wrinkle Formation

Catastrophic Collagen Loss: Women lose approximately 30% of their skin collagen in the first five years after menopause begins. Collagen, the building block of your skin composition, plays a huge role in maintaining healthy texture, appearance, and elasticity. This rapid loss causes skin to thin, sag, and wrinkle at an accelerated rate.

Declining Estrogen Impact: Estrogen stimulates collagen and elastin production. As estrogen levels plummet during perimenopause and menopause, your skin loses its ability to maintain firmness, bounce back from expressions, retain moisture effectively, and repair damage efficiently.

Compounding External Factors: Loss of collagen combined with decades of external factors like sun exposure, pollution, stress, and lifestyle choices causes menopausal skin to develop deeper wrinkles, more pronounced fine lines, significant sagging, and loss of facial volume.

This is why menopausal women need specialized natural ingredients that specifically address hormonal collagen loss and accelerated aging—not just surface-level treatments.

Vitamin E: Essential Antioxidant Protection

Coconut Oil: Deep Nourishment for Wrinkle Reduction

posts.content:
Your skin is one of the first things that changes when you enter those menopausal years. Looking after yourself, let alone your skin, can seem like a daunting challenge.

Your skin is likely to get drier, thinner, and more vulnerable to dark spots, melasma, and hyperpigmentation during this time. You'll experience the dreaded hot flashes, hormonal fluctuations, and frustrating pigmentation issues that seem impossible to fade. If you're looking for natural solutions that help combat menopausal hyperpigmentation but are unable to find ones that work, you may not have discovered the power of licorice extract yet.

Menopausal women commonly experience hyperpigmentation issues due to hormonal changes, sun damage accumulated over decades, and thinning skin that makes dark spots more visible. In this blog, we focus on licorice extract—a remarkable natural ingredient proven to tackle hyperpigmentation in menopausal skin—and explain the two most common pigmentation issues associated with menopause.

Understanding Hyperpigmentation in Menopause

Skin pigmentation changes can happen at any time throughout your life, but menopause dramatically accelerates these issues. The most common concern is hyperpigmentation, in which the skin darkens because melanin production increases in specific areas.

Numerous factors contribute to menopausal hyperpigmentation, including declining estrogen levels that disrupt melanin regulation, decades of accumulated sun exposure becoming visible, hormonal imbalances triggering melanocyte activity, thinning skin that makes pigmentation more pronounced, and slower cell turnover that prevents dark spots from fading naturally.

Melasma, sun spots, age spots, and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation from acne are among the disorders that can alter your skin during and after menopause. Moreover, the thinning of your skin barrier and collagen loss during menopause act as accelerants toward visible hyperpigmentation.

This is where licorice extract becomes essential for menopausal skin struggling with dark spots and uneven tone.

Why Licorice Extract Works for Menopausal Hyperpigmentation

Licorice extract, derived from the root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. For menopausal skin specifically, licorice extract offers unique benefits that address hyperpigmentation at multiple levels.

The Science Behind Licorice Extract

Glabridin—The Active Compound: The most powerful component in licorice extract is glabridin, which inhibits tyrosinase, the enzyme responsible for melanin production. By blocking this enzyme, licorice extract prevents new dark spots from forming and helps existing hyperpigmentation fade naturally.

Liquiritin—The Brightening Agent: Another key compound, liquiritin, actively disperses and removes existing melanin in the skin. This dual-action approach makes licorice extract particularly effective for stubborn menopausal hyperpigmentation that resists other treatments.

Melasma in Menopause: Licorice Extract as Your Solution

Melasma, appearing as dark patches on your face (typically on cheeks, forehead, upper lip, and chin), has been strongly connected to hormonal changes in women. Melanocytes—your skin's pigment-producing cells—create too much pigment, resulting in the development of melasma patches that can be frustrating and persistent.

How Licorice Extract Treats Menopausal Melasma

Licorice extract specifically addresses melasma through multiple mechanisms:

Tyrosinase Inhibition: By blocking the enzyme that creates melanin, licorice extract prevents melasma from darkening and spreading—essential for menopausal women whose hormonal fluctuations constantly trigger new pigmentation.

Existing Pigment Dispersal: Liquiritin in licorice extract actively breaks down and disperses existing melanin deposits, helping stubborn melasma patches gradually fade over time.

UV Protection Support: While not a sunscreen replacement, licorice extract's antioxidants provide additional defense against UV-triggered melanin production that worsens melasma in menopausal skin.

Inflammation Reduction: Melasma is often accompanied by inflammation, which can trigger more pigmentation. Licorice extract's anti-inflammatory properties break this cycle, allowing skin to heal and fade naturally.

Acne Scars and Post-Inflammatory Hyperpigmentation in Menopause

Due to the increased quantity of androgens compared to estrogen during menopause, hormonal acne frequently becomes a frustrating occurrence. Our skin's ability to replace old cells and create new ones slows dramatically as we age, meaning acne scars and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation linger far longer than they did in our younger years.


How Licorice Extract Heals Acne Hyperpigmentation

Prevents Dark Spot Formation: When applied during or immediately after a breakout, licorice extract inhibits the excess melanin production that typically occurs during skin inflammation, preventing dark marks from forming in the first place.

Fades Existing Marks: For stubborn acne scars and hyperpigmentation already present, licorice extract's liquiritin actively disperses melanin deposits, helping marks fade significantly faster than they would naturally—crucial for menopausal skin with slowed cell turnover.

Reduces Inflammation: By calming the inflammatory response associated with acne, licorice extract prevents the inflammation-pigmentation cycle that makes acne marks darker and more persistent in menopausal skin.

Supports Barrier Repair: Licorice extract helps restore the compromised skin barrier common in menopausal skin, creating optimal conditions for acne healing without excessive pigmentation.

posts.content:
Summer skincare pretty much steers us to the treasure of hydrating and nourishing practices our skin loves when the sun is blazing outside!

Get ready to give your skincare routine a summer revamp with our ultimate guide to DIY Hydrating Skincare!

If you're craving a change and want to pamper your skin with simple, clean, and organic ingredients, you've come to the right place. Our team at Empress Naturals has gathered their holy-grail 'kitchen to skin' DIY recipes and top hacks to help you make the most of your skincare goals in the summer.

We believe that a minimalistic yet effective skincare routine is all your skin needs, but a little extra TLC never hurts anyone. With summer fast approaching, it's the perfect time to refresh your skincare approach and embrace the natural goodness found in your own kitchen.

In this blog, we're spilling the secrets to achieving that coveted hydrated yet glowing complexion. Discover skin-friendly summer ingredients that will revolutionize your skincare routine, along with perfectly curated recipes for hydrating face masks, for ultimately soothed skin.

STOCK UP YOUR PANTRY FOR HYDRATED SKIN

We have a list of 7 must have ingredients you will find in all of our pantries the minute it starts getting hot outside and now you do too!

Plus, the abundance of recipes means you will never get bored of what you are using and your skin will experience a different reset each time!

Note: Ensure you patch test any DIY Recipe before you use it on your face.

Aloe Vera

Whether you choose to use fresh aloe or pure store bought, its benefits are incredible to rehydrate skin. Its high water content helps replenish and retain moisture in the skin. Aloe vera contains polysaccharides that act as natural humectants, attracting and locking in moisture to keep the skin hydrated.


Aloe vera gel forms a protective barrier on the skin, preventing moisture loss and maintaining hydration levels.

The gel also contains vitamins, antioxidants, and amino acids that nourish and promote healthy skin.Aloe vera can help improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles associated with dehydration.

Oatmeal

Have you ever wondered why people rave on about oatmeal baths? Oats contain beta-glucan, a polysaccharide that forms a protective barrier on the skin, helping to retain moisture and prevent water loss. The proteins in oatmeal have water-binding properties, allowing them to attract and hold moisture in the skin.


Oatmeal is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin E and phenols, which help protect the skin from environmental damage and promote hydration. They are also perfect for those with sensitive skin providing relief for various skin conditions, including eczema and psoriasis.


Rose Water

Rose water can be used as a facial mist or as an addition to DIY skincare recipes, providing a refreshing burst of hydration. It contains natural oils and sugars that help attract and retain moisture in the skin, effectively hydrating and moisturizing it.

Rose water acts as a humectant, helping to lock in moisture and prevent water loss from the skin. Rose water is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C, which help protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote hydration.

Spirulina

Spirulina is a type of blue-green algae that offers various benefits for rehydrating the skin. Spirulina has a unique ability to retain water, acting as a natural hydrating agent for the skin.

Spirulina contains phycocyanin, a pigment with anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe dry and irritated skin which is crucial for those with dry skin triggered by menopause.

It supports the production of collagen, a protein responsible for maintaining the skin's moisture and firmness. The algae's natural detoxifying properties also help remove impurities from the skin, allowing for better absorption of hydrating ingredients.


Cucumber Extract

Cucumber extract is known for its high water content, making it a natural hydrating ingredient for the skin.

It contains antioxidants and vitamins, such as vitamin C and caffeic acid, which help to replenish and retain moisture in the skin. It has natural astringent properties that can help tighten and firm the skin, promoting a more hydrated and rejuvenated appearance.


Olive Oil

Olive oil contains 60-85% linoleic acid which is an essential fatty acid that plays a crucial role in maintaining skin hydration. It helps to strengthen the skin's natural moisture barrier, preventing water loss and promoting optimal hydration levels.

Linoleic acid is also incredible to use for mature skin to bring back moisture into dry menopausal skin.

Linoleic acid supports the production of ceramides, which are essential for maintaining the skin's moisture balance. It is involved in the synthesis of skin lipids, including sebum, which contributes to the skin's natural hydration.

Manuka Honey

Manuka honey helps to create a protective barrier on the skin, preventing moisture loss and locking in hydration. It contains enzymes that promote skin cell renewal and regeneration, leading to a smoother and more hydrated complexion.

It is rich in antioxidants that help combat free radicals and protect the skin from environmental damage, promoting healthier and more hydrated skin.

If you are looking to add hydration to your skincare routine, we've got you covered! From CICA to Squalane, read all about the hydrating ingredients for your skin.

DIY HYDRATING FACE MASKS

Homemade so you know exactly what is hydrating your skin!

Spirulina and Manuka Honey Mask

Ingredients

  • 1 teaspoon spirulina powder
  • 1 tablespoon manuka honey
  • 1 teaspoon plain yogurt

Instructions

In a small bowl, combine the ingredients till a smooth paste. Apply the mask evenly to your face, avoiding the delicate eye area.

Leave the mask on for about 15-20 minutes to allow the ingredients to work their magic.

How it works

  1. Spirulina helps rehydrate the skin by attracting and retaining moisture.
  2. Manuka honey acts as a natural humectant, drawing moisture into the skin and creating a protective barrier to prevent moisture loss.
  3. Yogurt further enhances hydration, thanks to its lactic acid content that gently exfoliates and helps retain moisture.


Aloe-Rose Oatmeal Delight

Ingredients

  • 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel
  • 1 tablespoon of rose water
  • 2 tablespoons of finely ground oatmeal
  • 1 teaspoon of mashed avocado

Instructions

In a small bowl, combine the aloe vera gel and rose water. Gradually add the finely ground oatmeal and mashed avocado to the mixture.

Let the mask sit for a few minutes to allow the oatmeal to soften and absorb the liquid.

Leave the mask on for about 15-20 minutes.

How it works

  1. Aloe Vera soothes and calms inflammation, providing relief to dry and irritated skin.
  2. Rose water acts as a gentle toner, balancing the skin's pH levels.
  3. Oatmeal is a gentle exfoliant that removes dead skin cells, unclogs pores, and forms a protective barrier.
  4. Yogurt contains lactic acid, a natural exfoliant that gently balances and protects the skin's microbiome.


Hydrating Clay Mask

Ingredients

  • 2 tablespoons of clay powder (such as bentonite or kaolin clay)
  • 1 tablespoon of honey
  • 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel
  • 1 teaspoon of rose water
  • 2-3 drops of lavender essential oil

Instructions

Mix the clay powder with enough water to form a smooth paste.

Add the honey, aloe vera gel, rose water, and lavender essential oil and combine thoroughly

Leave the mask on for about 10-15 minutes, or until it starts to dry.


How it works

  1. This DIY clay face mask combines the hydrating properties of honey, aloe vera gel, rose water, and jojoba oil with the purifying and detoxifying effects of clay.
  2. It helps to replenish moisture, soothe the skin, and leave it feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.

posts.title: Amazing facts about Rosehip seed oil
posts.excerpt: Beauty is only skin-deep – the popular saying goes pointing on the innate beauty of character. You may think it’s the ultimate truth and...
posts.content:
Beauty is only skin-deep – the popular saying goes pointing on the innate beauty of character. You may think it’s the ultimate truth and may choose to go with the flow – busy, drifting towards where life takes you.

Of course, inner beauty is significant, but that doesn’t mean that what you are externally doesn’t count!

When a barrage of age signs intrudes into your skin, you can’t help but look bewildered! It’s high time to spruce up your old skin care regimen to save it from further damage. Give a glow-up time for your skin, too.

Rosehip seed oil is an amazing element incorporated into some of the most subtle skin-enhancing products because of its varied skin-healing properties. It’s a powerhouse of skin-friendly components.

Let’s know more about Rosehip seed oil. What is Rosehip seed oil?

This ‘Queen of all beauty oils’ is extracted from the seeds of the Rosa rubiginosa, seen in the southern regions of the Andes, mostly found in Chile or from Rosa canina, a wild rose species seen spread over Europe, western Asia, and the north-western sides of Africa.

Rose hip seed oil is pressed from the seeds of the rose plant. This oil can be used as a carrier oil, which can be effectively mixed and used with other products or intense essential oils due to its thin consistency and high skin-absorbing properties.

Rosehip seed oil penetrates your skin layers to balance the oil production, minimizing it, which in turn unclogs the pores. The appearances of age signs, blemishes, even stretch marks and scars look diminished.

Benefits of Rose hip seed oil for your skin

Rosehip seed oil brings a natural glow and even tone to the skin. It plays a vital role in fighting the age signs, boosting collagen production.

Keeps dry skin moisturized and supple

Being a non-greasy oil, it helps retain the moisture of the skin.

The essential fatty acids in the oil, which include linoleic (omega-6) and linolenic acid (omega-3), help to keep the cell walls strong enough to maintain the water balance of the skin.

Exfoliates the skin

Rosehip seed oil is a natural exfoliant. With ample amounts of Vitamin A, the oil helps reduce dryness and dullness in your skin, making it glowing and lively.

Repairs and heals the skin

Loaded with vitamins A, K, B1, B2, and B3, this oil has amazing healing and skin-repairing properties. The damage caused to the skin can be visibly reduced by the regular use of this oil.

Protection from sun damage

Ravages caused by constant UV exposure to the sun plays a pivotal role in premature aging and inhibit natural collagen production.

Rosehip oil contains antioxidants like vitamins A, C, and E, which synergistically fight visible sun damage and prevent photoaging. But as this can’t be used directly as a sunscreen, you need to consult your dermatologist before replacing this with your sunblock.

Skin regeneration, reduction of scars, and fine lines

The essential fatty acids and antioxidants in rosehip oil play a vital role in tissue and cell regeneration of the skin.

The oil is proven to be effective for skin healing, reduction of scars, fine lines, wrinkles, and crow’s feet.

Boosts skin elasticity and collagen production

This is the go-to oil for severely aged-looking skin. Rich in Vitamins A and C, necessary elements for the production of collagen, the oil significantly improves collagen production, thereby bringing back skin’s elasticity and suppleness.

Reduces skin inflammation

With good amounts of polyphenols and anthocyanins, the inflammation-reducing components, along with Vitamin E, an antioxidant with its anti-inflammatory effects, rosehip seed oil reduces skin inflammation and skin irritation considerably. Brightens and evens out the skin: Enriched with Vitamin C, the oil visibly brightens up your skin, making it vibrant and refreshed.

Reduces hyperpigmentation

The Vitamin A in rose hip seed oil is a rich source of several nutritional compounds like retinoids, which help reduce hyperpigmentation and other visible age signs of skin when used regularly

Reduces acne

Rosehip seed oil is known for its anti-microbial properties and low comedogenicity. It doesn’t clog the skin pores, thus reduces the menacing acne formation.

How to use Rosehip seed oil on your skin?

This oil is non-greasy and dry, and it gets quickly absorbed into the skin. The oil is usually safe for all types of skin, but it is advisable to go for a patch test to ensure that the oil doesn’t cause any allergies to your skin.

How to do the patch test:

  • Apply a little amount of the oil to your wrist
  • Cover the area with a gauze or a band-aid
  • Check after 24 hours for signs of irritation or inflammation
  • If your skin is irritated, itchy, or inflamed, you should not use rosehip seed oil. Consult your dermatologist if the irritation persists.
  • If there are no signs of irritation, the oil is safe to use.
  • After a successful patch test, you can use rosehip seed on your skin up to twice daily.
  • The oil can be applied on its own, used with your regular moisturizer or mixed with other potent essential oils for long-lasting effects.

UV rays can lessen the potency or make the rosehip seed oil rancid if exposed. So, a dark colored bottle is ideal to keep the product and has to be stored in a cool, dark place for extended shelf life.

Importance of cold-pressed, organic Rosehip seed oil for skin

Pure, organic cold-pressed Rosehip seed oil has long-lasting and outstanding effects on the skin. While using the cold-pressed technique, the oil is extracted from the seed, nut, and kernel of the plant at low temperatures.

This helps to prevent the decomposition of thermosensitive compounds in the oil, making it literally organic to safeguard your skin in the most natural way.

It’s imperative to choose clean beauty products that are 100% natural, organic with transparent labelling of all the ingredients used in their product development. Never choose low-quality or low-cost products that are compromised.

Your skin is unique and keeping it youthful, supple and radiant is an art. Read more about how to keep your skin healthy and youthful on our blogs.

posts.content:
The dreaded M-word: Menopause. Like most things in life, menopause can be surprising, confusing, and frustrating. Why? Well, because we lack the resources and knowledge required to deal with sudden changes that are expected but not anticipated.

Menopause is as natural a process as aging; something that cannot be eliminated, but definitely managed. If you're approaching the latter years of life, you’re probably finding that your skin and appearance are changing far more quickly than you want. But that doesn't mean there aren’t ways to maintain youthful-looking skin as you get older!

In recent years, anti-aging skincare has found new standing in the beauty industry. With every product claiming to be beneficial for mature or menopausal skin, it is extremely confusing for customers to find the perfect fit.

We have put together a list of the best and cleanest skincare items that will work on your skin, keeping in mind its type, age, and needs.


Do You Know What is Happening to Your Skin?

When you age, your skin transforms as a result of intrinsic or extrinsic factors. Your skin might age due to extrinsic factors like pollution, stress, and sun exposure.

On the other hand, the deterioration of collagen is one of the inherent processes that causes skin to age over time. As collagen is the protein that provides our skin strength and elasticity, long-term loss of it can make our skin look and feel older.

Your body stops producing estrogen as you enter menopause. Collagen and elastin, the two proteins necessary to keep your skin hydrated, healthy, and plump are produced by our bodies using estrogen. Hence, this sudden change in your skin’s composition results in thinning and brittle skin.

Some Signs to Look out for to Identify Menopausal Skin:

  • Dry, Flaky, and Itchy Skin
  • Fine Lines and Wrinkles
  • Hyperpigmentation and Dark Spots
  • Acne Breakouts or Hypersensitive Skin
  • Unwanted Facial Hair

Incorporate this tool into your regimen to alleviate muscular tension, eliminate toxins, decrease inflammation, oxygenate the skin, and diminish the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Through regular use and massage, the Kansa Wand revitalizes the skin, enhancing its natural glow and promoting a healthier, more vibrant appearance before make-up and skincare.
